hub_targets.title: Social cognition uses mirror modelling and coupling.

Play mode boundary requires a co-maintained context tag held by both agent and environment; agent-internal tagging alone is insufficient and unilateral play assertion without mutual signal is manipulation.
Play mode operates by substituting synthetic z_goal and z_harm signals while permitting full learning flow through E3/E2/E1 as if signals were real.
Mutual frame-maintenance signaling is necessary for play to function as distinct from manipulation: unilateral play declaration without partner agreement masks real harm as synthetic.
Play is a structurally necessary behavioral mode: without a bounded low-stakes learning context, strategy acquisition requires accepting real harm risk or forgoing novel exploration entirely.
Strategy and calibration dissociate at play episode close: policy structure transfers to real episodes; goal/harm magnitude calibration is re-anchored by real homeostatic and harm signals.
Minimal signalling channel requirements.
Language emergence and bootstrapping dynamics.
Language is a symbolic mediation and coordination layer.

created_claims.title: Language acquisition tracks play_mode maturation: socially-grounded compositional language emerges as a specialised game type instantiated within play_mode (INV-058 / MECH-194 / ARC-049), not via a parallel language-acquisition substrate. The basic expression catalog (play pose, head tilt, laughter, mutual gaze, distress vocalisation, approach cue, vocal mimicry, pointing) acts as the bilateral frame-opening signal; conversational violations function as synthetic z_harm; utterance-as-goal acts as synthetic z_goal; proto-symbols accrete as conditional modifiers attached to basic-expression channels. Predicts that systems trained without play_mode-like episodes fail to acquire socially-grounded compositional language even when pattern-matching imitation succeeds.

hub_targets.title: Hippocampal map nodes must store an explicit 4-component valence vector (wanting, liking, harm-discriminative, surprise) updated incrementally per visit; replay prioritisation is weighted by drive-state-gated valence relevance.

Inactive or strained anchors whose preserved goal payload still matches the current goal form a ranked ghost-goal bank; priority scales with wanting, goal-match, staleness, and recoverability rather than with staleness alone.
E1 prediction error surprise at moderate magnitudes generates intrinsic positive valence (curiosity/novelty drive); information-seeking is architecturally grounded.
e1_predictive_wanting
z_beta modulates E3 heartbeat frequency, distinct from precision-weighting.

Hippocampal replay is surprise-gated and generative: episodes with high unexplained prediction error are prioritized, replayed with counterfactual variations via E2, and deprioritized only when forward models can account for the surprise.

created_claims.title: Anticipatory positive affect (excitement) and anticipatory negative affect (dread) emerge as derived states from the conjunction of (a) signed VALENCE_SURPRISE, (b) MECH-216 schema readout coupled to z_beta arousal, (c) anticipatory VALENCE_LIKING write at predicted-imminent-positive-event locations, and (d) MECH-216 writes at predicted rather than current location. Four-gap substrate amendment to MECH-216 + MECH-205 + the residue valence buffer, NOT a new VALENCE channel.

hub_targets.title: Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).
affective_harm_hysteretic_integration

Cingulate integration substrate: a network-level module with AIC, dACC/aMCC, pMCC, pACC, and PCC analog subdivisions bound by a salience-network coordinator. Primary output: operating-mode variable plus behavioural-adjustment-magnitude signal.

created_claims.title: z_harm_a requires a controllability-modulated weighting term beyond MECH-219 temporal hysteresis: perceived uncontrollability or inescapability amplifies the affective load integrator, while perceived controllability suppresses it. Biological locus is dorsal ACC, which integrates noxious stimulus input with controllability context and projects to the medial suffering pathway (ACC/insula, overlapping with salience network).

hub_targets.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).

Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).
Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.

Context-bound passive safety representation: the agent encodes 'this environment predicts absence of suffering' through sustained non-occurrence of harm, without requiring a discrete teaching event. Substrate is prefrontal-hippocampal (IL for expression gating; vHipp-to-PL-equivalent for contextual store). Update rule is diffuse and slow -- accumulated context exposure without harm drives the store. Output lowers background vigilance threshold: reduces the general avoidance commitment level when the agent is in an environment the store predicts as safe. Parallel to the 'things liked' goal-cue store but tracking suffering-absence rather than reward-presence. Sister claim to MECH-304 (cue-specific conditioned inhibition pathway).

Relief-completion event reuses goal-achievement / reward-circuit completion machinery. When a comparator on a suffering-stream derivative crosses a downward threshold (sustained suffering-reduction below baseline), the resulting event fires the same downstream tag-and-release pipeline as goal-achievement: MECH-057a (commitment-release / beta-gate-drop), MECH-091 (salient-event phase-reset), and MECH-094 (categorical hypothesis-tag write gate). The polarity of the reinforcement is set at the input -- what counts as the completion is the suffering-derivative crossing, not a reward-onset event -- but the consolidation pipeline is shared. 'Things that just reduced suffering' are tagged in the same approach-attractor structure as 'things liked'.

created_claims.title: Cue-specific conditioned safety prediction: a discrete predictive structure encodes 'this stimulus actively predicts that suffering will be omitted', updated by explicit MECH-302 relief-completion events as its teaching signal. Encoding substrate is cortico-striatal (dorsal striatum + dlPFC). Expression/output pathway is IL-to-CeA. This is the conditioned-inhibition mechanism: when a specific cue co-occurs with relief, a separate learned prediction forms that can gate avoidance commitment release when the cue is later present. Sister claim to MECH-303 (contextual passive safety representation).

hub_targets.title: Two distinct goal-directed systems operate in parallel: a habit system (SNc/dorsal-striatum, model-free) sufficient for approach in familiar contexts, and a hippocampally-planned system (VTA/ventral-striatum+PFC, model-based) required for novel contexts, long-horizon benefit accumulation, and prosocial planning.

policy_hierarchy_dynamic_regranularisation (parent commitment for dynamic policy-primitive granularity in hippocampal rollout). Architectural commitment: the unit-of-policy that the rule-apprehension layer (ARC-062) and the diversity-generation layer (ARC-065) operate on is itself a dynamic representation, not a fixed primitive. The granularity rescales based on simulation outcomes -- decomposed into finer primitives when a chunk fails to ground at execution / prediction resolution (ARC-070), composed into a single primitive when a sequence has been repeatedly grounded with consistent outcomes (ARC-071). Without this commitment, ARC-007 hippocampal proposals are flat action sequences at fixed grain, and the rule layer must apprehend rules at whatever grain the proposer happens to emit, which may be too fine (combinatorial explosion in rollout) or too coarse (chunks fail to map onto the actual state). Cluster parent for ARC-070 (decomposition) + ARC-071 (composition); registers the architectural commitment that primitives are dynamic before any mechanism work.

created_claims.title: policy_composition_via_repeated_grounding (zoom-out / chunk a sequence of primitives into a single atomic primitive after repeated successful execution). Child of ARC-069 dynamic-regranularisation parent commitment. Architectural commitment: sequences of policy primitives that have been repeatedly executed together with consistent outcomes are accumulated into a single primitive that the rule-apprehension and diversity-generation layers can treat atomically. Trigger: repetition count + outcome consistency (slow accumulator). Direction: zoom out. Phenomenology anchor: 'making coffee was once seventeen motor acts; after a thousand repetitions it is one unit.' Names the TRANSITION mechanism (planned -> habitual chunk) that MECH-163 dual systems presupposes but does not specify.

hub_targets.title: policy_hierarchy_dynamic_regranularisation (parent commitment for dynamic policy-primitive granularity in hippocampal rollout). created_claims.title: policy_decomposition_on_prediction_failure (zoom-in / re-segment imagined chains when chunks fail to ground at execution resolution). Child of ARC-069 dynamic-regranularisation parent commitment. Architectural commitment: when an imagined chunk's predicted outcome diverges from what its sub-elements would predict (or when MECH-269 V_s on the chunk's region drops), the chunk is broken into finer primitives in the rollout proposal stream. Trigger: simulation-side prediction failure. Direction: zoom in. Phenomenology anchor: 'I planned go-to-the-kitchen; turns out I need to plan the steps.'

policy_hierarchy_dynamic_regranularisation (parent commitment for dynamic policy-primitive granularity in hippocampal rollout). opportunity_cost_no_op_penalty (waiting accumulates forgone-reward cost; passive trajectories pay for time consumed). Architectural commitment: trajectories that consume time without progress are not free in E3 scoring; the score function carries an additive cost on no-op / repeated-stationary candidates that scales with the agent's average expected reward rate. Companion to ARC-066 (capacity-to-vigor) and ARC-067 (idle aversion). Existing dACC switch_value (SD-032b / Kolling 2015 foraging) is the closest current substrate but covers a different framing (when to switch FROM the current option to alternatives), not the always-on cost of choosing the null action.

idle_aversion_boredom (sustained-low-engagement aversive valence accumulator). Architectural commitment: prolonged low-engagement is itself negatively valenced; the agent acts to escape boredom, even in the absence of specific targets, threats, or novelty pressure. Distinct from the policy-side ARC-066 vigor coupling: ARC-067 instantiates an aversive-side accumulator that recruits the same downstream as actual harm (z_harm-like channel), so engagement-poverty competes for action-selection priority on the same axis as discomfort. Companion to ARC-066 (vigor / capacity-deploy) and ARC-068 (opportunity cost) in the non_deficit_action_drives family.

tonic_vigor_coupling (capacity-to-action-bias, average-reward-rate / LC-NE tonic / DA effort-vigor analog). Architectural commitment: behaviour-bias toward action scales with capacity (high energy AND low recent prediction error AND low drive -- 'things are going well, deploy'), independent of any specific target. Distinct from existing behavioural-source slots (z_harm threats, z_goal deficit-seeded wants, novelty curiosity) which all fall to zero when the agent is well-fed, safe, and the world is familiar. Without ARC-066 a healthy REE agent has no gradient to act; biology has a positive gradient (felt phenomenology: drive to deploy energy when one has it). First of three slots in the non_deficit_action_drives family; companion ARC-067 (idle aversion / boredom) and ARC-068 (opportunity cost) cover the aversive-of-rest and cost-of-passivity halves of the same architectural commitment. Family principle: behaviour comes from surplus capacity AND from deficits, not deficits alone.

behavioral_diversity_generation_pathway (multi-substrate distributed: LC-NE tonic + frontopolar curiosity + striatal novelty + hippocampal trajectory sampling). Architectural commitment: a non-trivial behavioural diversity generation pathway is upstream-of and logically prior to both rule pathways (ARC-062 top-down rule-apprehension and ARC-064 bottom-up rule-discovery). Without it, neither pathway has anything to weight or cluster -- gradient descent on a parametric policy without diversity pressure collapses to the smoothest single regime, the predicted equilibrium MECH-309 identifies. The biology distributes diversity generation across at least four substrates: LC-NE tonic noise floor, frontopolar uncertainty-driven curiosity, striatal novelty bonus, and hippocampal trajectory sampling. REE registers this as a foundational architectural slot whose specific child mechanisms (MECH-313 noise-floor, MECH-314 structured-curiosity with three sub-flavours, and the MECH-315-absorbed proposal-diversity channel) are independently testable but jointly load-bearing.

Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

Closure operator over committed rule_state: on satisfaction of a committed rule_state's completion condition, a 'done' token releases the bistable commitment latch (MECH-090), imposes a temporary No-Go bias (MECH-260) on the just-completed action-class / rule, and discharges the residue associated with that commitment within its domain. The closure token is what turns evaluation off; its absence leaves the governance loop stuck in evaluate-more even when the rule is satisfied.

hub_targets.title: Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.
z_gamma split into z_self (E2 motor-sensory domain) and z_world (E3/Hippocampus domain).
Goal-directed navigation requires a dedicated z_resource encoder that captures object-type features invariant to spatial location, separate from z_world.

created_claims.title: Multi-resource heterogeneity: qualitatively distinct resource types (e.g. food / water / novelty) with per-type identity tags, type-specific spawn distributions, type-specific benefit profiles, and a per-axis homeostatic drive system, plus a curriculum-introduction hook. Provides the goal-identity vocabulary the wanting/liking + identity-distinct-goal cohort (MECH-229/230, MECH-117/216, ARC-030/032, Q-030, SD-015 unblock chain) needs to be honestly tested at substrate level, and the substrate scaffold for downstream developmental-schedule work.

hub_targets.title: Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).
Directional limb damage: body extension for genuine z_harm_s / z_harm_a causal independence

Shared forward-model trunk for aversive prediction: a single HarmForwardTrunk substrate encodes a modality-independent unsigned prediction error shared across aversive streams (z_harm_s, z_harm_a, potentially others); stream-specific HarmForwardHeads read out signed per-stream predictions. COMPETES WITH ARC-033, which commits to independent per-stream forward models.

created_claims.title: Interoceptive noise dynamics: stochastic body-state perturbations independent of the agent's motor output (autonomic background noise + sensitisation spikes + fatigue drift) provide an agent-independent interoceptive background dense enough for the affective comparator (ARC-058, Level 2 of the reafference comparator family) to be honestly tested at substrate level.

hub_targets.title: Temporoparietal junction (TPJ) acts as agency-detection comparator distinguishing self-caused from other-caused change.
Single-pass agency attribution via forward-model comparator on the z_harm_s reafferent stream: residual = z_harm_s_observed - E2_harm_s(z_harm_s_{t-1}, a_actual).
Directional limb damage: body extension for genuine z_harm_s / z_harm_a causal independence

created_claims.title: Multi-source environmental dynamics: concurrent stochastic event sources at distinct spatial and temporal scales (spatial perturbation field + transient Poisson events + background drift sources) provide an agent-independent causal background dense enough for agency-detection comparators (MECH-095) and reafference cancellation (MECH-098) to be honestly tested at substrate level.

hub_targets.title: Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.
Goal-directed navigation requires a dedicated z_resource encoder that captures object-type features invariant to spatial location, separate from z_world.

Inactive or strained anchors whose preserved goal payload still matches the current goal form a ranked ghost-goal bank; priority scales with wanting, goal-match, staleness, and recoverability rather than with staleness alone.

dACC/aMCC-analog (adaptive control): module that reads z_harm_a precision-weighted prediction error, z_conflict, and control-demand signals; outputs behavioural-adjustment magnitude; writes to a learnable striatal-analog action-value target.

Waking hippocampal search allocates a minority probe budget to top ghost-goal entries, seeding proposals around unresolved but still-wanted anchors instead of relying on pure current-anchor CEM or random probe noise.

created_claims.title: Multi-slot GoalState holds N>=2 simultaneously-active goal slots with per-slot z_goal, drive coupling, age, and persistence parameters. Each slot generates its own ghost-goal-bank rank, ghost-probe budget, and proposer trajectories; a dACC-style arbitrator selects which slot's best trajectory commits this tick.

hub_targets.title: Action objects as hippocampal map backbone, enabling long-horizon planning beyond step-level trajectories.
Three BG-like cortico-striatal loops require distinct learning channels.

At trajectory completion, HippocampalModule performs a backward temporal credit sweep that propagates the terminal outcome (goal contact or failure) retroactively through preceding states, consistent with the reverse replay observed at reward endpoints in awake rats (Foster & Wilson 2006).

created_claims.title: Action-chunk cache stores frequently-traversed action sequences as cached lookups, indexed by (state-signature, expected-outcome-signature). Sits in the dorsolateral-loop slot of ARC-021. Provides the model-free habit pathway missing from V3 and the runaway-chunking failure mode underlying OCD compulsions and rituals (Graybiel 2008).

created_claims.title: Motor primitive substrate at the bottom of the action-representation hierarchy: a small fixed dictionary of force-field-like primitive activations that vectorially combine to produce the action_object that E2 consumes. Required only when REE moves to continuous limb-like control.

hub_targets.title: vmPFC is the substrate that converts stored affective, normative, and residue content -- prior outcome history, social and identity constraints, learned value associations, and safety memories -- into active components of the navigable state used by E3 during trajectory evaluation.

Inactive or strained anchors whose preserved goal payload still matches the current goal form a ranked ghost-goal bank; priority scales with wanting, goal-match, staleness, and recoverability rather than with staleness alone.

PFC subdivision architecture: a network-level module with lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal), OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure map), vmPFC-analog (value integration), premotor/SMA-analog (sequence execution), and frontopolar-analog (branching / nested control; V4 scope) subdivisions. Primary output: the rule/policy context that gates action selection and the natural write target for MECH-261 mode-conditioned gating.

created_claims.title: vmPFC analogue gains an abstract task-structure encoding capacity that compresses anchor-pool geometry into a generalisable structural code, preserving similarity geometry across surface-different but structurally-matched problems. Without this, REE cannot exhibit Baram et al. 2020 task-structure abstraction signatures.

Three BG-like cortico-striatal loops require distinct learning channels.

Inactive or strained anchors whose preserved goal payload still matches the current goal form a ranked ghost-goal bank; priority scales with wanting, goal-match, staleness, and recoverability rather than with staleness alone.

created_claims.title: Option library substrate stores a discrete codebook of named, reusable action subroutines (each as initiation-set + termination-function + internal-policy triple, per Sutton-Precup-Singh 1999), distinct from the existing continuous SD-004 action_object_decoder. Required for hierarchical action composition, tool use, social-coordination protocols, and richer ghost-probe seeding.

hub_targets.title: Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

created_claims.title: An explicit thalamic-routing substrate (reuniens/MD-analogue) gates and amplifies bidirectional traffic between hippocampal anchor pool and frontal substrates. Currently abstracted into the GoalState->HippocampalModule call chain; making it explicit is needed when V3 dynamics demonstrate that the implicit routing is insufficient.

Dual-trace anchors preserve a goal-state snapshot plus wanting/arousal payload at write or invalidation time, so unresolved goals remain queryable after the active plan fails.

created_claims.title: Type-encoder produces a category-prototype feature vector that is written into the AnchorSet alongside z_world, so anchors can be queried by type-key as well as location-key. The type-encoder uses the same input substrate as the existing instance-encoder but produces a different (regularity-extracted) representation, mirroring the trisynaptic-vs-monosynaptic complementary-learning architecture within hippocampus.

hub_targets.title: Hippocampal systems store and replay paths through residue-field terrain.

Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).
Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.

Cue-specific conditioned safety prediction: a discrete predictive structure encodes 'this stimulus actively predicts that suffering will be omitted', updated by explicit MECH-302 relief-completion events as its teaching signal. Encoding substrate is cortico-striatal (dorsal striatum + dlPFC). Expression/output pathway is IL-to-CeA. This is the conditioned-inhibition mechanism: when a specific cue co-occurs with relief, a separate learned prediction forms that can gate avoidance commitment release when the cue is later present. Sister claim to MECH-303 (contextual passive safety representation).

created_claims.title: Context-bound passive safety representation: the agent encodes 'this environment predicts absence of suffering' through sustained non-occurrence of harm, without requiring a discrete teaching event. Substrate is prefrontal-hippocampal (IL for expression gating; vHipp-to-PL-equivalent for contextual store). Update rule is diffuse and slow -- accumulated context exposure without harm drives the store. Output lowers background vigilance threshold: reduces the general avoidance commitment level when the agent is in an environment the store predicts as safe. Parallel to the 'things liked' goal-cue store but tracking suffering-absence rather than reward-presence. Sister claim to MECH-304 (cue-specific conditioned inhibition pathway).

hub_targets.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).

created_claims.title: Relief-completion event reuses goal-achievement / reward-circuit completion machinery. When a comparator on a suffering-stream derivative crosses a downward threshold (sustained suffering-reduction below baseline), the resulting event fires the same downstream tag-and-release pipeline as goal-achievement: MECH-057a (commitment-release / beta-gate-drop), MECH-091 (salient-event phase-reset), and MECH-094 (categorical hypothesis-tag write gate). The polarity of the reinforcement is set at the input -- what counts as the completion is the suffering-derivative crossing, not a reward-onset event -- but the consolidation pipeline is shared. 'Things that just reduced suffering' are tagged in the same approach-attractor structure as 'things liked'.

hub_targets.title: Inactive or strained anchors whose preserved goal payload still matches the current goal form a ranked ghost-goal bank; priority scales with wanting, goal-match, staleness, and recoverability rather than with staleness alone.

Waking hippocampal search allocates a minority probe budget to top ghost-goal entries, seeding proposals around unresolved but still-wanted anchors instead of relying on pure current-anchor CEM or random probe noise.

MECH-271 routing is state-gated. In waking, the anchor channel dominates the hippocampal fan-out: replay preferentially reaches consolidation and viability-map consumers to support decision-making. In sleep, the probe channel dominates: replay preferentially seeds Bayesian schema restructuring -- testing which action-outcome attributions held up across episodes, creating new buckets for non-fitting experiences, revising causal graphs. The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

Sleep-consolidation priority from V_s residuals: during sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation (MECH-275) and sleep-dependent self-model aggregation (MECH-273), the order and weight of replay events is biased by the schema-staleness map produced by MECH-284. Replay is not uniform across schema and is not solely salience-driven (dopaminergic tagging); it is also prioritised by accumulated verisimilitude residuals. Schema regions with the most accumulated 'this is wrong' evidence get replayed first, more often, and with higher schema-update authority during sleep. PTSD-rumination has a clean architectural account here: MECH-285 priority loss co-occurring with MECH-094 hypothesis-tag dysfunction, locking the staleness tag from ever clearing.

created_claims.title: Priority-weighted anchor replay (MECH-285) runs not only during sleep but also during brief waking quiescent pauses, surfacing remote / non-current stored content for goal-directed retrieval. The architectural gating is contextual (quiescent vs active behaviour, sensory input vs absence) rather than state-categorical (waking vs sleep). Refinement / generalisation of MECH-285.

Theta-cycle content scales with the agent's current substrate abstraction vocabulary: the unit packaged into a single theta cycle is the smallest reusable item available in the active substrate stack -- atomic actions when only those exist, action chunks when SD-045 lands, type-instance matches when SD-040/MECH-296 land, option invocations when SD-042 lands. Refinement of MECH-089 (theta-gamma nesting) on the content-of-packet axis.

created_claims.title: Cognitive map traversal via theta sequences operates at the agent's active abstraction level. Theta sequences do not have a fixed semantic content -- they simulate trajectories through whatever cognitive map the substrate vocabulary currently defines, whether spatial, conceptual, type-graph, or option-graph. Sister claim to MECH-299, on the map-being-traversed axis.

hub_targets.title: Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

Event-segment detection: a substrate-side two-level hierarchical detector that emits monotonic outer.inner segment IDs on detected event boundaries, feeding region-keyed substrates (anchor sets per MECH-269, V_s readouts per Phase 1, staleness accumulators per MECH-284) and the MECH-287 broadcast trigger at the schema/event-segment scale per Eichenbaum 2017 / Sols-DuBrow-Davachi 2017 / Baldassano 2017. Default substrate: PE-threshold detector on the fast scale (z_world+z_self) and BOCPD-Gaussian latent change-point on the slow scale (z_goal); MECH-287 broadcast fires downstream of MECH-288 boundaries (Clewett 2025) rather than via an independent comparator. The segment_id field is the region key consumed by the V_s invalidation runtime cluster; without it, MECH-269 anchor sets and per-region V_s aggregation have no substrate-native source for the region partition.

OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure cognitive map): module that represents the state space of the current task and the specific outcomes expected from actions in each state. E2's natural substrate address for harm-predictive rollouts; distinct from scalar value integration (SD-033c).

created_claims.title: Event-gated frontal write at goal-instantiation moments: when GoalState transitions from inactive to active, or when z_goal changes substantially, perform a high-bandwidth one-shot transfer of LatentStack content into SD-033a rule_state and SD-033b state_code, replacing the slow-EMA update for that tick. Mirrors Spellman et al. 2015 encoding-direction HPC->PFC dependence at goal-cue encoding.

Runs both during waking (moment-to-moment type recognition) and during sleep (consolidation refinement of the type-anchor pool).

V_s residual schema-staleness accumulator: a region-indexed scalar that integrates broadcast invalidation events (MECH-287) with attribution_weight credit assignment over the active anchor set, leaks slowly, and is read by both online anchor-reset (MECH-269 hysteresis check, waking) and sleep-consolidation priority (MECH-285, offline). Distinct from generic prediction error (which operates on latent values and updates online via gradient): MECH-284 is region-indexed, accumulates across events, and exposes a dual read-out (online + offline). Refined 2026-04-22 from sleep-only formulation to dual-readout formulation after V3-EXQ-475 demonstrated that an agent trapped in a stale anchor needs an online consumer of the staleness signal -- MECH-285 sleep-only readout cannot rescue the agent during waking. Saturated map produces over-attention to schema revision (anxiety-analog); absent map produces schema rigidity (V3-EXQ-475 phenotype: 5-6 PAG releases per seed but never integrate to anchor invalidation -> 1000/1000 freeze-active steps).
e1_predictive_wanting

E3 maintains a structured latent goal representation (positive attractor in z_goal sub-space, measurable as z_goal_norm > 0) distinct from harm avoidance, enabling hippocampal terrain navigation toward goal states.

created_claims.title: Dual-trace anchors preserve a goal-state snapshot plus wanting/arousal payload at write or invalidation time, so unresolved goals remain queryable after the active plan fails.

hub_targets.title: Quiescent E3 heartbeat cycles trigger hippocampal SWR-equivalent replay for viability map consolidation.

The hippocampal SWR trajectory generator has anti-recency weighting: infrequently experienced trajectories are proposed more often than recently committed ones, and novel-path sequences (never behaviorally executed) are actively generated from available path segments.

created_claims.title: HippocampalModule CEM trajectory sampling must include an anti-recency exploration bonus that down-weights recently committed trajectory segments, ensuring the proposal distribution covers all available paths rather than converging on recency-weighted routes.

hub_targets.title: Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.

Salience-network coordinator: network-level module reading AIC-, dACC-, MCC-, PCC-analog subdivisions and outputting a discrete operating-mode variable (external_task / internal_replay / internal_planning / offline_consolidation) plus a mode-switch trigger.

GABAergic regulatory layer applies cross-stream exponential decay (z_harm_s, z_harm_a, z_beta) modulated by tonic gaba_tone; missing-decay regulator is the proximal substrate cause of harm-stream lock-in (catatonia subtype II) observed in V3-EXQ-471.

PAG-analog gates the freeze response: freeze commit when z_harm_a x duration exceeds threshold; exit requires z_harm_a to fall below an exit_threshold scaled by gaba_tone; failure of exit produces sustained immobility characteristic of stuporous catatonia.

created_claims.title: Broadcast override regulator (orexin-analog) is the third regulatory layer of the V3 control stack: a small, broadly-projecting gain modulator that integrates internal homeostatic state and threat context to produce an override_signal that reweights z_harm at downstream commit gates and gates the SD-012 drive->z_goal seeding bridge. Dissociable from SD-036 (GABA decay) and from MECH-186/187/188 (5-HT goal-pipeline gain): each layer predicts a clinically distinct failure phenotype (catatonia subtype II / depression / narcolepsy + PWS-hyperphagia). SD-036 alone is architecturally insufficient to break harm-stream lock-in under sustained threat; SD-037 supplies the override authority that lets metabolic demand drive z_goal seeding when threat persists.

created_claims.title: Closure operator over committed rule_state: on satisfaction of a committed rule_state's completion condition, a 'done' token releases the bistable commitment latch (MECH-090), imposes a temporary No-Go bias (MECH-260) on the just-completed action-class / rule, and discharges the residue associated with that commitment within its domain. Broadcast override regulator (orexin-analog) is the third regulatory layer of the V3 control stack: a small, broadly-projecting gain modulator that integrates internal homeostatic state and threat context to produce an override_signal that reweights z_harm at downstream commit gates and gates the SD-012 drive->z_goal seeding bridge. SD-036 alone is architecturally insufficient to break harm-stream lock-in under sustained threat; SD-037 supplies the override authority that lets metabolic demand drive z_goal seeding when threat persists.

Dual-component online invalidation trigger: an UPSTREAM anchor-side comparator (CA1/CA3 mismatch flowing through subicular routing per Vinogradova 2001 + O'Mara 2009) coupled to a DOWNSTREAM broadcast event (LC phasic burst per Aston-Jones-Cohen 2005, with parallel signals from generalised dopaminergic prediction error per Schultz 1997 and lateral habenula negative-RPE per Matsumoto-Hikosaka 2007), wired together by the HC-VTA loop (Lisman & Grace 2005). The two components are coupled stages of one trigger event, not alternatives. Distinct from MECH-284 the accumulator: MECH-287 is the discrete trigger event (anchor-side mismatch + broadcast release) that drives accumulation. Triggered by latent-stream prediction failure (norm(z_pred(t-1) - z_obs(t)) > theta_trigger) OR strongly negative value-PE (value_PE(t) < -theta_value) OR exogenous surprise. Carries no schema-region credit on its own; downstream MECH-284 attributes the broadcast event to the active anchor set. Failure modes dissociate from MECH-284: trigger-loss produces single-event blindness (no anchor ever feels invalidated, but staleness map remains queryable); accumulator-loss produces V3-EXQ-475's phenotype directly (releases happen but never integrate). Failure modes also dissociate from MECH-285 sleep priority: trigger-loss prevents staleness from accruing, while sleep-priority loss prevents accrued staleness from being repaired. Within MECH-287 itself, anchor-side comparator failure dissociates from broadcast failure: comparator-loss = no upstream signal to broadcast (silent trigger, biologically a CA1/CA3 lesion); broadcast-loss = comparator fires but no global gain modulation downstream (silent invalidation, biologically an LC lesion).

policy_decomposition_on_prediction_failure (zoom-in / re-segment imagined chains when chunks fail to ground at execution resolution). Child of ARC-069 dynamic-regranularisation parent commitment. Architectural commitment: when an imagined chunk's predicted outcome diverges from what its sub-elements would predict (or when MECH-269 V_s on the chunk's region drops), the chunk is broken into finer primitives in the rollout proposal stream. Trigger: simulation-side prediction failure. Direction: zoom in. Phenomenology anchor: 'I planned go-to-the-kitchen; turns out I need to plan the steps.'

created_claims.title: Event-segment detection: a substrate-side two-level hierarchical detector that emits monotonic outer.inner segment IDs on detected event boundaries, feeding region-keyed substrates (anchor sets per MECH-269, V_s readouts per Phase 1, staleness accumulators per MECH-284) and the MECH-287 broadcast trigger at the schema/event-segment scale per Eichenbaum 2017 / Sols-DuBrow-Davachi 2017 / Baldassano 2017. Default substrate: PE-threshold detector on the fast scale (z_world+z_self) and BOCPD-Gaussian latent change-point on the slow scale (z_goal); MECH-287 broadcast fires downstream of MECH-288 boundaries (Clewett 2025) rather than via an independent comparator. The segment_id field is the region key consumed by the V_s invalidation runtime cluster; without it, MECH-269 anchor sets and per-region V_s aggregation have no substrate-native source for the region partition.

MECH-271 routing is state-gated. In waking, the anchor channel dominates the hippocampal fan-out: replay preferentially reaches consolidation and viability-map consumers to support decision-making. In sleep, the probe channel dominates: replay preferentially seeds Bayesian schema restructuring -- testing which action-outcome attributions held up across episodes, creating new buckets for non-fitting experiences, revising causal graphs. The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

Sleep-consolidation priority from V_s residuals: during sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation (MECH-275) and sleep-dependent self-model aggregation (MECH-273), the order and weight of replay events is biased by the schema-staleness map produced by MECH-284. Replay is not uniform across schema and is not solely salience-driven (dopaminergic tagging); it is also prioritised by accumulated verisimilitude residuals. Schema regions with the most accumulated 'this is wrong' evidence get replayed first, more often, and with higher schema-update authority during sleep. PTSD-rumination has a clean architectural account here: MECH-285 priority loss co-occurring with MECH-094 hypothesis-tag dysfunction, locking the staleness tag from ever clearing.

SD-036 alone is architecturally insufficient to break harm-stream lock-in under sustained threat; SD-037 supplies the override authority that lets metabolic demand drive z_goal seeding when threat persists.

Orexin-analog gain modulation of drive-coupled arousal (motor-coupling axis): an orexin-analog modulator gates the coupling between drive_level and arousal-relevant downstream targets (PFC for SD-033a deliberation, BLA/CeA for SD-035 amygdala arbitration, beta-gate for MECH-090). Loss of this coupling produces a cataplexy-analog failure where emotional/drive input fails to recruit motor activation -- z_harm and drive_level compute normally but action selection does not integrate them. This is the motor-coupling axis of orexin function; the wake-stability axis (sleep-state gating, the dominant biological role) is MECH-286. Both axes are downstream of the same SD-037 override_signal, predicting clinical co-occurrence of cataplexy with sleep-state instability under orexin loss (narcolepsy).

created_claims.title: Override-gated sleep-state transition: SD-037 override_signal also gates entry to and exit from offline (sleep) mode. High override (sustained drive + threat under metabolic demand) suppresses sleep-mode onset; low override + accumulated MECH-284 V_s-residual staleness + permissive threat context permits onset and recruits the MECH-285 sleep-replay priority readout. Architecturally this is the recruitment authority for the offline arm of the V_s bidirectional cluster: MECH-284 accumulates the demand for offline schema repair, MECH-285 sets the priority order, and MECH-286 provides the on-switch. Without MECH-286, MECH-285 produces a priority list with no transition mechanism to consume it. This is the V3 architectural account of orexin's canonical role as wake-stability gate (Saper/Scammell flip-flop), which MECH-281 (drive-arousal coupling) understates by framing cataplexy purely as a motor-coupling failure.

hub_targets.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).
Hippocampal systems store and replay paths through residue-field terrain.

Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

V_s residual schema-staleness accumulator: a region-indexed scalar that integrates broadcast invalidation events (MECH-287) with attribution_weight credit assignment over the active anchor set, leaks slowly, and is read by both online anchor-reset (MECH-269 hysteresis check, waking) and sleep-consolidation priority (MECH-285, offline). Distinct from generic prediction error (which operates on latent values and updates online via gradient): MECH-284 is region-indexed, accumulates across events, and exposes a dual read-out (online + offline). Refined 2026-04-22 from sleep-only formulation to dual-readout formulation after V3-EXQ-475 demonstrated that an agent trapped in a stale anchor needs an online consumer of the staleness signal -- MECH-285 sleep-only readout cannot rescue the agent during waking. Saturated map produces over-attention to schema revision (anxiety-analog); absent map produces schema rigidity (V3-EXQ-475 phenotype: 5-6 PAG releases per seed but never integrate to anchor invalidation -> 1000/1000 freeze-active steps).

MECH-271 routing is state-gated. In waking, the anchor channel dominates the hippocampal fan-out: replay preferentially reaches consolidation and viability-map consumers to support decision-making. In sleep, the probe channel dominates: replay preferentially seeds Bayesian schema restructuring -- testing which action-outcome attributions held up across episodes, creating new buckets for non-fitting experiences, revising causal graphs. The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

Sleep-phase full-Bayesian aggregation is a general mechanism across attribution domains: given a waking-phase feedstock of counterfactual-backed attributions (produced by MECH-276), sleep phases aggregate across many episodes, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases that single-episode attribution cannot catch. MECH-273 (self-model aggregation) and MECH-274 (other-model aggregation, V4) are specialisations. Object-schema revision is a further specialisation. INV-049 (offline phases mathematically necessary for model-building agents) is operationalised here: what sleep is mathematically necessary FOR is this cross-episode Bayesian aggregation of counterfactual-backed attribution.

The self-model has a waking half and a sleep half. The waking half is SD-003 (single-episode counterfactual self-attribution via E2). The sleep half is full-Bayesian aggregation of single-episode SD-003 outputs across many episodes, routed by the anchored channel to E1 and SD-033a, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases. Without the sleep half, REE has an episode-local causal signature but not a durable self-model.

created_claims.title: Sleep-consolidation priority from V_s residuals: during sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation (MECH-275) and sleep-dependent self-model aggregation (MECH-273), the order and weight of replay events is biased by the schema-staleness map produced by MECH-284. Replay is not uniform across schema and is not solely salience-driven (dopaminergic tagging); it is also prioritised by accumulated verisimilitude residuals. Schema regions with the most accumulated 'this is wrong' evidence get replayed first, more often, and with higher schema-update authority during sleep. PTSD-rumination has a clean architectural account here: MECH-285 priority loss co-occurring with MECH-094 hypothesis-tag dysfunction, locking the staleness tag from ever clearing.

Dual-component online invalidation trigger: an UPSTREAM anchor-side comparator (CA1/CA3 mismatch flowing through subicular routing per Vinogradova 2001 + O'Mara 2009) coupled to a DOWNSTREAM broadcast event (LC phasic burst per Aston-Jones-Cohen 2005, with parallel signals from generalised dopaminergic prediction error per Schultz 1997 and lateral habenula negative-RPE per Matsumoto-Hikosaka 2007), wired together by the HC-VTA loop (Lisman & Grace 2005). The two components are coupled stages of one trigger event, not alternatives. Distinct from MECH-284 the accumulator: MECH-287 is the discrete trigger event (anchor-side mismatch + broadcast release) that drives accumulation. Triggered by latent-stream prediction failure (norm(z_pred(t-1) - z_obs(t)) > theta_trigger) OR strongly negative value-PE (value_PE(t) < -theta_value) OR exogenous surprise. Carries no schema-region credit on its own; downstream MECH-284 attributes the broadcast event to the active anchor set. Failure modes dissociate from MECH-284: trigger-loss produces single-event blindness (no anchor ever feels invalidated, but staleness map remains queryable); accumulator-loss produces V3-EXQ-475's phenotype directly (releases happen but never integrate). Failure modes also dissociate from MECH-285 sleep priority: trigger-loss prevents staleness from accruing, while sleep-priority loss prevents accrued staleness from being repaired. Within MECH-287 itself, anchor-side comparator failure dissociates from broadcast failure: comparator-loss = no upstream signal to broadcast (silent trigger, biologically a CA1/CA3 lesion); broadcast-loss = comparator fires but no global gain modulation downstream (silent invalidation, biologically an LC lesion).

created_claims.title: Verisimilitude recognition-for-recall gate: V_s computed at retrieval time (not proposal time) gates whether a stored episode is even a candidate for re-entry into the proposal stream. Recognition (does this fit my current situation?) is logically prior to anchor-eligibility (is this a good place to start a rollout from?). MECH-269's V_s is computed against the proposed anchor state; MECH-283's V_s is computed against the retrieved episode content. Loss of MECH-283 produces intrusive recall -- episodes that don't fit current context get pulled into the proposal stream (PTSD flashback / schizophrenic loose-association phenomenology).

hub_targets.title: Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).
Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.
Amygdala analogue as BLA + CeA salience stage between z_harm_a and the cingulate/hippocampus — two peer non-trainable arithmetic modules owning the MECH-046 and MECH-074a-d behaviours.

Broadcast override regulator (orexin-analog) is the third regulatory layer of the V3 control stack: a small, broadly-projecting gain modulator that integrates internal homeostatic state and threat context to produce an override_signal that reweights z_harm at downstream commit gates and gates the SD-012 drive->z_goal seeding bridge. Dissociable from SD-036 (GABA decay) and from MECH-186/187/188 (5-HT goal-pipeline gain): each layer predicts a clinically distinct failure phenotype (catatonia subtype II / depression / narcolepsy + PWS-hyperphagia). SD-036 alone is architecturally insufficient to break harm-stream lock-in under sustained threat; SD-037 supplies the override authority that lets metabolic demand drive z_goal seeding when threat persists.

PAG-analog gates the freeze response: freeze commit when z_harm_a x duration exceeds threshold; exit requires z_harm_a to fall below an exit_threshold scaled by gaba_tone; failure of exit produces sustained immobility characteristic of stuporous catatonia.

created_claims.title: LPB interoceptive routing into harm-arbitration: a lateral-parabrachial-nucleus analog (LPB) routes interoceptive distress signals (visceral malaise, taste-aversion analogs, hypoxia, hypoglycemia) into the harm-arbitration network via PBN-CGRP -> CeA -> PAG. This is the upstream broadcast arm of the threat-side architecture, parallel to VMHdm's predator-side broadcast. Both feed downstream arbiters where SD-037's override_signal reweights them. Architectural commitment: interoceptive harm has a separable input channel from external-threat z_harm, dissociable in pathology and in normal modulation.

hub_targets.title: Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.

Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.
BG-level beta oscillations gate E3-to-action-selection propagation, not E3 internal updating.

created_claims.title: Orexin-analog gain modulation of drive-coupled arousal (motor-coupling axis): an orexin-analog modulator gates the coupling between drive_level and arousal-relevant downstream targets (PFC for SD-033a deliberation, BLA/CeA for SD-035 amygdala arbitration, beta-gate for MECH-090). Loss of this coupling produces a cataplexy-analog failure where emotional/drive input fails to recruit motor activation -- z_harm and drive_level compute normally but action selection does not integrate them. This is the motor-coupling axis of orexin function; the wake-stability axis (sleep-state gating, the dominant biological role) is MECH-286. Both axes are downstream of the same SD-037 override_signal, predicting clinical co-occurrence of cataplexy with sleep-state instability under orexin loss (narcolepsy).

created_claims.title: LH-PAG override projection: a direct projection from LH (orexin and/or peri-LC GABA analog) to PAG modulates the GABAergic freeze gate (MECH-279) under metabolic demand. The projection does not silence the freeze gate; it raises its threshold (theta_freeze_effective = theta_freeze_base x (1 + alpha_override x override_signal)). This produces the behavioural transition from freeze to active foraging under sustained hunger plus threat -- the foraging-under-predation phenotype the lit-pull surveys.

hub_targets.title: z_gamma split into z_self (E2 motor-sensory domain) and z_world (E3/Hippocampus domain).
Action objects as hippocampal map backbone, enabling long-horizon planning beyond step-level trajectories.

The scientist-agent principle: the waking-phase mechanism that feeds MECH-275 is deliberate intervention. The agent generates hypotheses (proposer-side, via MECH-269 probe channel), selects actions that will produce informative contrast under those hypotheses, executes the actions, observes the outcomes, and computes counterfactual-backed attribution. Correlational observation is insufficient -- the agent must behave like a scientist, performing experiments whose outcomes produce structured attribution signals. Applies across all three developmental stages; specialised by MECH-277 (action-space discovery), MECH-278 (object-schema formation), and a V4 social-intervention mechanism (parallel to MECH-278 for other-agents).

Stage-1 specialisation of MECH-276: the agent discovers its own action space via motor experimentation (motor babbling) before SD-003 counterfactual self-attribution is well-formed. The agent performs motor acts and observes which sensory changes covary with them; the output is the substrate SD-003 requires -- a set of actions the agent has reason to believe are self-initiated and for which it has counterfactual expectations. Without MECH-277, the counterfactual action space (a_cf) is not well-defined and SD-003 degenerates.

REE developmental training proceeds in three ordered stages: (1) self-as-object — action-space discovery via motor experimentation, producing the substrate SD-003 requires; (2) objects-as-patterns — object-schema formation via experimental action on the world; (3) others-as-special-objects — modelling other agents as objects with their own policies. The ordering is load-bearing: without a self-model the agent cannot distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change; without object-schemas the agent cannot recognise other-agents as a distinguished subclass of objects. Refines the generic ARC-019 curriculum-gates claim.

created_claims.title: Stage-2 specialisation of MECH-276: once the agent can distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change (stage 1 complete via MECH-277), it forms object-schemas via experimental action on the world. An object is the stable bundle of features that behave causally together under interventional perturbation -- push here, these features move together; occlude this, that cluster disappears. Object-schema formation is the stage-2 specialisation of the scientist-agent principle, with world-features as the target of intervention. V3's z_world (SD-005) is currently engineered pre-split; this is an architectural shortcut past stage 2.

Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.

The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

Without the sleep half, REE has an episode-local causal signature but not a durable self-model.

MECH-094's hypothesis tag is realized as differential downstream routing of hippocampal replay, not as a source-side boolean. Anchored replay (trusted starting state, MECH-269) preferentially reaches subiculum -> entorhinal deep layers -> neocortex (E1 consolidation) and lateral PFC (SD-033a viability map); probe replay (low-verisimilitude seed, strengthened tag) preferentially reaches BLA (affective tagging without consolidation) and NAc (curiosity / novelty scaffolding). Tag loss presents as probe content reaching consolidation destinations it should not reach -- an auditable confabulatory-planning signature distinct from psychosis or hallucination.

MECH-271 routing is state-gated. In waking, the anchor channel dominates the hippocampal fan-out: replay preferentially reaches consolidation and viability-map consumers to support decision-making. In sleep, the probe channel dominates: replay preferentially seeds Bayesian schema restructuring -- testing which action-outcome attributions held up across episodes, creating new buckets for non-fitting experiences, revising causal graphs. The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

Sleep-phase full-Bayesian aggregation is a general mechanism across attribution domains: given a waking-phase feedstock of counterfactual-backed attributions (produced by MECH-276), sleep phases aggregate across many episodes, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases that single-episode attribution cannot catch. MECH-273 (self-model aggregation) and MECH-274 (other-model aggregation, V4) are specialisations. Object-schema revision is a further specialisation. INV-049 (offline phases mathematically necessary for model-building agents) is operationalised here: what sleep is mathematically necessary FOR is this cross-episode Bayesian aggregation of counterfactual-backed attribution.

The self-model has a waking half and a sleep half. The waking half is SD-003 (single-episode counterfactual self-attribution via E2). The sleep half is full-Bayesian aggregation of single-episode SD-003 outputs across many episodes, routed by the anchored channel to E1 and SD-033a, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases. Without the sleep half, REE has an episode-local causal signature but not a durable self-model.

created_claims.title: V4-reserved. The sleep-dependent aggregation pattern of MECH-273 extends to other-attribution. Fast empathy (ARC-010) produces single-episode attributions of other-agent action-outcome links; a stable model of other minds requires full-Bayesian aggregation of those attributions during sleep, architecturally parallel to MECH-273 but operating on other-agent rather than self attributions.

hub_targets.title: Hippocampal systems store and replay paths through residue-field terrain.

Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.
Self-attribution via counterfactual E2: causal signature = E2(z_t, a_actual) minus E2(z_t, a_counterfactual).

Sleep-consolidation priority from V_s residuals: during sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation (MECH-275) and sleep-dependent self-model aggregation (MECH-273), the order and weight of replay events is biased by the schema-staleness map produced by MECH-284. Replay is not uniform across schema and is not solely salience-driven (dopaminergic tagging); it is also prioritised by accumulated verisimilitude residuals. Schema regions with the most accumulated 'this is wrong' evidence get replayed first, more often, and with higher schema-update authority during sleep. PTSD-rumination has a clean architectural account here: MECH-285 priority loss co-occurring with MECH-094 hypothesis-tag dysfunction, locking the staleness tag from ever clearing.

MECH-094's hypothesis tag is realized as differential downstream routing of hippocampal replay, not as a source-side boolean. Anchored replay (trusted starting state, MECH-269) preferentially reaches subiculum -> entorhinal deep layers -> neocortex (E1 consolidation) and lateral PFC (SD-033a viability map); probe replay (low-verisimilitude seed, strengthened tag) preferentially reaches BLA (affective tagging without consolidation) and NAc (curiosity / novelty scaffolding). Tag loss presents as probe content reaching consolidation destinations it should not reach -- an auditable confabulatory-planning signature distinct from psychosis or hallucination.

Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

V_s residual schema-staleness accumulator: a region-indexed scalar that integrates broadcast invalidation events (MECH-287) with attribution_weight credit assignment over the active anchor set, leaks slowly, and is read by both online anchor-reset (MECH-269 hysteresis check, waking) and sleep-consolidation priority (MECH-285, offline). Distinct from generic prediction error (which operates on latent values and updates online via gradient): MECH-284 is region-indexed, accumulates across events, and exposes a dual read-out (online + offline). Refined 2026-04-22 from sleep-only formulation to dual-readout formulation after V3-EXQ-475 demonstrated that an agent trapped in a stale anchor needs an online consumer of the staleness signal -- MECH-285 sleep-only readout cannot rescue the agent during waking. Saturated map produces over-attention to schema revision (anxiety-analog); absent map produces schema rigidity (V3-EXQ-475 phenotype: 5-6 PAG releases per seed but never integrate to anchor invalidation -> 1000/1000 freeze-active steps).
SD-003 counterfactual attribution requires a dedicated harm forward model E2_harm_s(z_harm_s, action) -> z_harm_s_next operating within the sensory-discriminative harm stream.

hub_targets.title: dACC/aMCC-analog (adaptive control): module that reads z_harm_a precision-weighted prediction error, z_conflict, and control-demand signals; outputs behavioural-adjustment magnitude; writes to a learnable striatal-analog action-value target.

dACC-analog bias suppression against recency / monostrategy: the dACC module writes a counter-bias that resists repeating the last-rewarded trajectory when long-term value information demands exploration. Candidate explanation of the monostrategy failure observed in fishtank_viz and earlier queue-diversity results.

z_harm_a enters action selection as a precision-weighted prediction error (harm_surprise_PE, SD-020) against an internal pain forward model (E2_harm_a), not as a raw magnitude. Upgrades SD-020 from provisional to prerequisite for SD-032b (dACC-analog).

Closure operator over committed rule_state: on satisfaction of a committed rule_state's completion condition, a 'done' token releases the bistable commitment latch (MECH-090), imposes a temporary No-Go bias (MECH-260) on the just-completed action-class / rule, and discharges the residue associated with that commitment within its domain. The closure token is what turns evaluation off; its absence leaves the governance loop stuck in evaluate-more even when the rule is satisfied.

created_claims.title: dACC conflict saturation: the dACC-analog (SD-032b) prediction-error / conflict signal caps and habituates under repeated identical outcomes; it does not grow unboundedly as conflict persists. Without saturation, a stuck governance loop can generate unbounded pe signal that never triggers a mode change, which is the control-theory signature of persistent rumination.

hub_targets.title: Quiescent E3 heartbeat cycles trigger hippocampal SWR-equivalent replay for viability map consolidation.
Action objects as hippocampal map backbone, enabling long-horizon planning beyond step-level trajectories.

Mode-conditioned write gating: the operating-mode variable from the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) determines which substrates can write to E3, episodic memory, policy updates, and autonomic coupling. Generalises MECH-094 hypothesis tag to a mode-conditioned family of write gates. Operating mode is implemented as a soft probability vector over {external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation}, not a one-hot categorical.

Salience-network coordinator: network-level module reading AIC-, dACC-, MCC-, PCC-analog subdivisions and outputting a discrete operating-mode variable (external_task / internal_replay / internal_planning / offline_consolidation) plus a mode-switch trigger.

created_claims.title: Mode-conditioned hippocampal trajectory proposals: HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories must condition on operating_mode (SD-032a) in addition to world_state. External_task mode proposes task-relevant trajectories; internal_planning mode proposes counterfactual / branching trajectories; internal_replay mode replays past-successful trajectories; offline_consolidation mode proposes stability-check trajectories. Without mode conditioning, replay content contaminates external-task selection.

hub_targets.title: PFC subdivision architecture: a network-level module with lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal), OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure map), vmPFC-analog (value integration), premotor/SMA-analog (sequence execution), and frontopolar-analog (branching / nested control; V4 scope) subdivisions. Primary output: the rule/policy context that gates action selection and the natural write target for MECH-261 mode-conditioned gating.

Salience-network switch threshold: when the precision-weighted salience of any input to the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) exceeds a threshold, the coordinator fires a whole-system mode switch rather than producing an incremental policy update.

created_claims.title: Asymmetric mode hysteresis in SalienceCoordinator: entering an operating mode uses a different threshold on the aggregate salience vector than exiting it, and the asymmetry is mode-specific. Schmitt-trigger extension of the existing SD-032a enter-threshold logic (currently enter-only / symmetric).

e1_predictive_wanting
Dual-trace anchors preserve a goal-state snapshot plus wanting/arousal payload at write or invalidation time, so unresolved goals remain queryable after the active plan fails.

Waking hippocampal search allocates a minority probe budget to top ghost-goal entries, seeding proposals around unresolved but still-wanted anchors instead of relying on pure current-anchor CEM or random probe noise.

created_claims.title: A good agent needs both a continuous wanting landscape and a discrete bank of unresolved goal traces: field-level wanting supports smooth local guidance, while ghost-goal traces preserve blocked or deferred goals whose local gradient has collapsed.

hub_targets.title: z_gamma split into z_self (E2 motor-sensory domain) and z_world (E3/Hippocampus domain).
Self-attribution via counterfactual E2: causal signature = E2(z_t, a_actual) minus E2(z_t, a_counterfactual).
REE requires staged developmental training with explicit curriculum gates.
Social cognition uses mirror modelling and coupling.

The scientist-agent principle: the waking-phase mechanism that feeds MECH-275 is deliberate intervention. The agent generates hypotheses (proposer-side, via MECH-269 probe channel), selects actions that will produce informative contrast under those hypotheses, executes the actions, observes the outcomes, and computes counterfactual-backed attribution. Correlational observation is insufficient -- the agent must behave like a scientist, performing experiments whose outcomes produce structured attribution signals. Applies across all three developmental stages; specialised by MECH-277 (action-space discovery), MECH-278 (object-schema formation), and a V4 social-intervention mechanism (parallel to MECH-278 for other-agents).

Stage-1 specialisation of MECH-276: the agent discovers its own action space via motor experimentation (motor babbling) before SD-003 counterfactual self-attribution is well-formed. The agent performs motor acts and observes which sensory changes covary with them; the output is the substrate SD-003 requires -- a set of actions the agent has reason to believe are self-initiated and for which it has counterfactual expectations. Without MECH-277, the counterfactual action space (a_cf) is not well-defined and SD-003 degenerates.

Stage-2 specialisation of MECH-276: once the agent can distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change (stage 1 complete via MECH-277), it forms object-schemas via experimental action on the world. An object is the stable bundle of features that behave causally together under interventional perturbation -- push here, these features move together; occlude this, that cluster disappears. Object-schema formation is the stage-2 specialisation of the scientist-agent principle, with world-features as the target of intervention. V3's z_world (SD-005) is currently engineered pre-split; this is an architectural shortcut past stage 2.

V4-reserved. The sleep-dependent aggregation pattern of MECH-273 extends to other-attribution. Fast empathy (ARC-010) produces single-episode attributions of other-agent action-outcome links; a stable model of other minds requires full-Bayesian aggregation of those attributions during sleep, architecturally parallel to MECH-273 but operating on other-agent rather than self attributions.

created_claims.title: REE developmental training proceeds in three ordered stages: (1) self-as-object — action-space discovery via motor experimentation, producing the substrate SD-003 requires; (2) objects-as-patterns — object-schema formation via experimental action on the world; (3) others-as-special-objects — modelling other agents as objects with their own policies. The ordering is load-bearing: without a self-model the agent cannot distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change; without object-schemas the agent cannot recognise other-agents as a distinguished subclass of objects. Refines the generic ARC-019 curriculum-gates claim.

hub_targets.title: Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

Mode-conditioned write gating: the operating-mode variable from the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) determines which substrates can write to E3, episodic memory, policy updates, and autonomic coupling. Generalises MECH-094 hypothesis tag to a mode-conditioned family of write gates. PFC subdivision architecture: a network-level module with lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal), OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure map), vmPFC-analog (value integration), premotor/SMA-analog (sequence execution), and frontopolar-analog (branching / nested control; V4 scope) subdivisions. Primary output: the rule/policy context that gates action selection and the natural write target for MECH-261 mode-conditioned gating.

Frontopolar counterfactual-value tracking: SD-033e maintains, in parallel with SD-033c's chosen-option value signal, a running estimate of the value of unchosen alternatives. A switch-to-alternative decision is signalled when the counterfactual estimate exceeds the chosen estimate by a threshold-sensitive margin.

Frontopolar relative-importance monitoring across competing goals: SD-033e maintains a representation of relative importance across a set of active goals (parallel, not pairwise), enabling parallel-goal monitoring and flexible switching. Lesion analogue should reproduce the Mansouri 2015 over-focus + novel-rule-impairment phenotype.

created_claims.title: Frontopolar-analog (parallel-goal deliberation substrate): V4-scope module maintaining counterfactual-value estimates for alternatives to the current course of action, mediating transitions between external engagement and internally-generated deliberation, enabling disengagement from the current task to explore novel alternatives, and monitoring relative importance across multiple active goals. Hooks reserved in V3 via the operating_mode vocabulary; substrate deferred to V4.

hub_targets.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).

vmPFC arbitration continuously weights model-based (E3/deliberative) vs. model-free (E1/habitual) contributions to action selection on a spectrum, not a binary switch. Commitment (beta-elevated E3) shifts weighting toward model-based without silencing the habitual channel; unexpected threat (urgency signal) shifts weighting toward model-free. The MECH-091 urgency interrupt is the substrate implementation of this arbitration shift, not a bypass of the beta gate.

Cingulate integration substrate: a network-level module with AIC, dACC/aMCC, pMCC, pACC, and PCC analog subdivisions bound by a salience-network coordinator. Primary output: operating-mode variable plus behavioural-adjustment-magnitude signal.

created_claims.title: pACC-autonomic coupling: module connecting affective-pain stream (z_harm_a) to valence/drive update pathway. Provides the affective-pain -> autonomic/visceral coupling that Vogt 2005 and Craig 2009 locate in perigenual/subgenual ACC.

hub_targets.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).
Quiescent E3 heartbeat cycles trigger hippocampal SWR-equivalent replay for viability map consolidation.

Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.

dACC-analog bias suppression against recency / monostrategy: the dACC module writes a counter-bias that resists repeating the last-rewarded trajectory when long-term value information demands exploration. hub_targets.title: A content class that is stored and retrievable in the REE system is not thereby active in the navigable state used for trajectory evaluation; active participation requires a preparation substrate (vmPFC-analog) that converts stored content into live state components at evaluation time.

A system with correct post-hoc ethical scoring but without an active constraint preparation substrate will produce the EVR pattern: correct verbal moral judgments coexisting with unconstrained trajectory generation; this pattern is not correctable by improving post-hoc scoring accuracy.

An agent that only avoids harm but cannot sustain prospective goal-directed motivation cannot exercise genuine agency; goal maintenance is a necessary co-condition for ethical agency alongside harm-avoidance.

hub_targets.title: Action objects as hippocampal map backbone, enabling long-horizon planning beyond step-level trajectories.
E1 prediction error surprise at moderate magnitudes generates intrinsic positive valence (curiosity/novelty drive); information-seeking is architecturally grounded.

There exists an optimal range of daily Model Error Load (MEL): insufficient MEL (extreme monotony, isolation, institutional under-stimulation) produces progressive model rigidity via under-stimulation of the learning drive even when sleep architecture is intact; excessive MEL (acute trauma, overwhelming novelty, extreme responsibility) produces overload insomnia and incomplete update even with extended sleep opportunity.

DA-modulated RBF center density: dopaminergic signal at reward encounters allocates multiple closely-spaced RBF centers in the hippocampal residue field, creating representational expansion at reward locations without requiring environmental richness.

Approach behavior toward reward locations emerges from the interaction of DA-mediated representational expansion (MECH-232) and an information-seeking (curiosity) drive operating on the hippocampal map. No explicit approach gradient or wanting field is required in hippocampal terrain.

created_claims.title: Curiosity drive: information-seeking bias in hippocampal trajectory scoring that favors regions of higher representational density in the RBF map.
