(b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

Mode-conditioned hippocampal trajectory proposals: HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories must condition on operating_mode (SD-032a) in addition to world_state. External_task mode proposes task-relevant trajectories; internal_planning mode proposes counterfactual / branching trajectories; internal_replay mode replays past-successful trajectories; offline_consolidation mode proposes stability-check trajectories. Without mode conditioning, replay content contaminates external-task selection.

PCC-analog (attention partition / metastability): module coordinating transitions between external-task and internal (replay / planning / consolidation) modes; contributes a mode-stability parameter to the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a).

Mode-conditioned write gating: the operating-mode variable from the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) determines which substrates can write to E3, episodic memory, policy updates, and autonomic coupling. Generalises MECH-094 hypothesis tag to a mode-conditioned family of write gates. Operating mode is implemented as a soft probability vector over {external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation}, not a one-hot categorical.

The simultaneous multi-instance, multi-scale, multi-time phenomenology of Type 3 dreams is the experiential signature of NREM SWR episodic-to-semantic transfer: rapid hippocampal sequential presentation of concept instances collapses to phenomenological simultaneity, with self-dissolution reflecting the stripping of episodic context during abstraction.

CA1 acts as a PE-proportional comparator that writes episodes to the surprise buffer: mismatch between CA3 prediction and entorhinal actual input produces a graded signal that tags the episode for offline replay and suppresses retrieval of the now-erroneous prediction.

Hippocampal replay is surprise-gated and generative: episodes with high unexplained prediction error are prioritized, replayed with counterfactual variations via E2, and deprioritized only when forward models can account for the surprise.

REE-v3 requires a minimal sleep-phase infrastructure: an SWS-analog phase (bidirectional hippocampus-to-cortex replay for schema/slot consolidation) and a REM-analog phase (causal attribution replay for slot-filling from co-correlational evidence ordering). Without these offline phases, hippocampal attribution mapping cannot converge and context representations remain globally undifferentiated.

Context slot-formation (schema abstraction: which distinctions between contexts are worth representing) and slot-filling (causal attribution: which evidence populates each slot) are computationally separated functions that cannot be co-computed in a single online pass. Slot structure must be consolidated during an SWS-analog phase before slot-filling during a REM-analog phase can yield reliable attribution signal.

Offline replay (MECH-121) must sample trajectory-diverse content -- including non-dominant and counterfactual paths -- to maintain multi-strategy viability; consolidation of only the dominant trajectory produces monostrategy in E1's world model even when SHY (MECH-120) is intact.

Bidirectional hippocampus-neocortex information flow is architecturally necessary for attribution mapping convergence: the SWS-direction (hippocampus to cortex) installs context schema structure; waking and REM-direction reactivations fill those structures with causal attribution estimates. Without bidirectional flow, context representations remain globally undifferentiated despite locally coherent online encoding.

When harm-trace salience dominates offline replay content, consolidation selectively amplifies harm predictions, progressively contracting option space -- a distinct failure mode from Q-021 behavioral flatness.
The REM sub-phase of offline mode recalibrates precision priors and tests world model hypotheses via unconstrained generative simulation, with commit gate fully suppressed.

source_claims.title: Should the running-variance / dynamic-precision update fire at action-selection time (early, control-plane-coupled), at outcome-integration time (late, residue-coupled, current location), or at both? Equivalently: is the StepHarness contract that requires experiment loops to call agent.update_residue() each step sufficient, or does ARC-016 precision need a call site that is structurally guaranteed to fire whenever the agent is selecting actions?

Does REE require a unified meta-level threshold supervisor that adapts pass/fail and commit/release thresholds across substrates from system-wide instability metrics, or do the existing scattered adaptive loci (ARC-016 dynamic precision, SD-032c AIC interoceptive baseline EMA, SD-032d PCC stability scalar, SD-032e pACC drive bias) produce coherent meta-stability under chronic load without a supervisor?

Sleep-phase full-Bayesian aggregation is a general mechanism across attribution domains: given a waking-phase feedstock of counterfactual-backed attributions (produced by MECH-276), sleep phases aggregate across many episodes, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases that single-episode attribution cannot catch. MECH-273 (self-model aggregation) and MECH-274 (other-model aggregation, V4) are specialisations. Object-schema revision is a further specialisation. INV-049 (offline phases mathematically necessary for model-building agents) is operationalised here: what sleep is mathematically necessary FOR is this cross-episode Bayesian aggregation of counterfactual-backed attribution.

V4-reserved. The sleep-dependent aggregation pattern of MECH-273 extends to other-attribution. Fast empathy (ARC-010) produces single-episode attributions of other-agent action-outcome links; a stable model of other minds requires full-Bayesian aggregation of those attributions during sleep, architecturally parallel to MECH-273 but operating on other-agent rather than self attributions.

The self-model has a waking half and a sleep half. The waking half is SD-003 (single-episode counterfactual self-attribution via E2). The sleep half is full-Bayesian aggregation of single-episode SD-003 outputs across many episodes, routed by the anchored channel to E1 and SD-033a, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases. Without the sleep half, REE has an episode-local causal signature but not a durable self-model.

Mode-conditioned write gating: the operating-mode variable from the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) determines which substrates can write to E3, episodic memory, policy updates, and autonomic coupling. Generalises MECH-094 hypothesis tag to a mode-conditioned family of write gates. Operating mode is implemented as a soft probability vector over {external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation}, not a one-hot categorical.

REM sleep, with varied associative replay and attenuated noradrenergic tone, consolidates template-performance prediction error into updates of z_goal -> attentional-template projection weights (feature-specification update), not into stored-goal content.
SWS hippocampal-neocortical replay consolidates goal-value prediction error into updates of stored goal representations (content update), not into attentional-template parameters.

Type 4 (detailed story, close to reality, super interesting) dreams are the phenomenological signature of REM E1 world-model free-running: with sensory input disconnected and the commit gate suppressed, E1's LSTM generates unconstrained sequential rollouts from its learned prior, producing coherent narrative arcs that are novel-but-plausible and experientially interesting.

Elevated tonic noradrenergic activity (from chronic stress, stimulant exposure, or noradrenaline reuptake inhibitor antidepressants) suppresses REM sleep by maintaining locus coeruleus REM-off firing above the silence threshold required for REM generation, thereby blocking MECH-123 precision recalibration and producing progressive behavioural rigidity equivalent to partial MECH-123 deprivation -- independent of any pharmacological REM suppression.
Play is a structurally necessary behavioral mode: without a bounded low-stakes learning context, strategy acquisition requires accepting real harm risk or forgoing novel exploration entirely.

recurrent_dependency_hubs.title: Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.

Frame confusion (synthetic->real direction): the agent treats simulation, replay, and imagination as real, producing delusion formation, PTSD flashbacks, and anxiety.

Novel environments and high-MEL learning episodes adaptively upregulate the learning drive component of sleep (INV-050 third drive), producing measurable increases in slow-wave activity power, sleep spindle density, and hippocampal replay rate proportional to the novelty and prediction error load encountered during the preceding wake period.

Prediction error type determines which sleep phase is demanded for processing: episodic, spatial, and factual errors (incorrect world-state predictions) primarily demand NREM-mediated schema replay (MECH-121) for prior correction; emotional, social, and identity-threatening errors primarily demand REM-mediated simulation and recalibration (MECH-123) for policy and salience update.

GABAergic sleep aids (benzodiazepines and Z-drugs: zolpidem, zopiclone, zaleplon) suppress SWS depth and continuity at therapeutic doses, impairing MECH-120 synaptic homeostasis and MECH-121 schema replay, producing context representations that remain globally undifferentiated -- the same failure mode as SD-017 without any SWS-analog.

Sleep architecture restoration in early MCI should produce dissociated cognitive improvement: slot-filling (episodic attribution, MECH-121 analog) should improve before slot-formation (contextual semantic, MECH-120 analog), because the pipeline rebuilds in dependency order. This distinguishes the attribution account from accounts predicting uniform memory improvement with sleep restoration.

Progressive sleep deprivation or neurodegeneration causes staged failure of the offline consolidation pipeline in reverse dependency order: precision recalibration (MECH-123/Phase 4) degrades first, producing early MCI features; slot-filling (MECH-121/Phase 2-3) next, producing the classic early Alzheimer's episodic profile (intact remote, absent new); slot-formation (MECH-120/Phase 1-2) last, producing middle-stage contextual identity loss and schema destabilisation; global context collapse last.

Offline replay (MECH-121) must sample trajectory-diverse content -- including non-dominant and counterfactual paths -- to maintain multi-strategy viability; consolidation of only the dominant trajectory produces monostrategy in E1's world model even when SHY (MECH-120) is intact.

Sleep phase architecture is regulated by three distinct drives -- circadian timing, homeostatic synaptic pressure, and a learning/model-update demand drive proportional to daily prediction error accumulation (Model Error Load, MEL) -- and only the third drive determines whether the overnight update phase is sufficient for the error burden generated during waking.

Any pharmacological agent that disrupts sleep architecture produces equivalent attribution pipeline degradation to behavioral sleep deprivation, proportional to which phase(s) are disrupted and the duration of exposure -- the pipeline is mechanism-agnostic and responds only to phase fidelity.

The biological NREM-then-REM sleep phase sequence is the correct computational order, derivable from the logical dependencies between the failure modes each phase addresses; this ordering is not a contingent biological fact but an engineering necessity, and it serves directly as the V4 rewiring specification: each phase specifies what structural change the REE substrate requires to address its failure mode.

recurrent_dependency_hubs.title: The NREM sub-phase of offline mode replays recent episodic content via SWR-equivalent sequences, transferring episodic traces from E3/hippocampal buffer to E1/neocortical schematic representation.

source_claims.title: pfc_context_bias_as_hippocampal_retrieval_gate -- PFC active goal/task representations send top-down bias signals that steer which hippocampal trajectory templates are retrieved or replayed; different PFC context states should produce different hippocampal retrieval subsets, enabling context-appropriate behavioral option selection; without this routing, the trajectory library is queried uniformly regardless of goal state.

Descending pain modulation: when E3 is committed to a trajectory through expected harm, z_harm_s PE precision is reduced (endogenous analgesia). Commitment gates sensory harm attenuation.

z_harm_a encodes affective surprise (precision-weighted PE), not raw accumulated harm state. The affective stream training target is the mismatch between predicted and actual harm accumulation, making E3 urgency respond to unexpectedness of threat, not just magnitude.

Ready vigilance (MECH-026) requires an arousal regulator that maintains an optimal sensitivity level on the LC-NE inverted-U curve, implemented via MECH-093 heartbeat frequency modulation rather than a binary high/low precision switch.

External vs internal cognition modes are controlled by precision-routing configurations on the same E1 substrate: External (high sensory, low hippocampal, low rollout), Internal (low sensory, high hippocampal, high rollout), Mixed (medium), and Replay (very low sensory, very high hippocampal, structured rollout). This dimension is orthogonal to the Action/Vigilance/Pathological modes (MECH-025-027).

The cue-indexed E1 context vector (MECH-150) projects to a terrain_weight signal [w_harm, w_goal] that scales E3's harm and goal scoring, implementing contextual precision modulation on the harm/benefit terrain evaluation.
High-precision action mode carries responsibility attribution: the precision level at which an action was committed determines the degree of ethical accountability assigned to its consequences.

Context-dependent neuromodulatory gain is a single unifying mechanism expressed at multiple levels: ARC-016 (temporal precision from prediction error variance), MECH-152 (contextual terrain weight from cue-indexed retrieval), MECH-026 (arousal-mediated vigilance sensitivity), and MECH-093 (z_beta heartbeat frequency modulation) are all facets of a global arousal/precision signal (LC-NE) modulated by a context-specific gain signal (vmPFC/OFC).
The REM sub-phase of offline mode recalibrates precision priors and tests world model hypotheses via unconstrained generative simulation, with commit gate fully suppressed.
The SWS sub-phase of offline mode performs global attractor flattening and residue field denoising (synaptic homeostasis analog), pruning weak noisy traces and restoring signal-to-noise ratio.
D_eff (z_self participation ratio) is a necessary co-condition for commitment, alongside world-side running_variance; both must be in range before irreversible action.
REE agents require second-order epistemic access to their own model confidence, structurally represented and wired into commit gating, not just observable from performance metrics.

recurrent_dependency_hubs.title: Modes are control-plane regimes applied to shared predictive machinery: the precision-to-commitment circuit.

source_claims.title: Theta-burst packet contains multi-content joint binding: each ~125ms theta cycle binds a {goal_latent, action_proposal, risk_estimate, state_summary} tuple within one phase-aligned window, with verisimilitude grounding the rollout step that must stay ahead of real time.

Waking hippocampal search allocates a minority probe budget to top ghost-goal entries, seeding proposals around unresolved but still-wanted anchors instead of relying on pure current-anchor CEM or random probe noise.

Dual-component online invalidation trigger: an UPSTREAM anchor-side comparator (CA1/CA3 mismatch flowing through subicular routing per Vinogradova 2001 + O'Mara 2009) coupled to a DOWNSTREAM broadcast event (LC phasic burst per Aston-Jones-Cohen 2005, with parallel signals from generalised dopaminergic prediction error per Schultz 1997 and lateral habenula negative-RPE per Matsumoto-Hikosaka 2007), wired together by the HC-VTA loop (Lisman & Grace 2005). The two components are coupled stages of one trigger event, not alternatives. Distinct from MECH-284 the accumulator: MECH-287 is the discrete trigger event (anchor-side mismatch + broadcast release) that drives accumulation. Triggered by latent-stream prediction failure (norm(z_pred(t-1) - z_obs(t)) > theta_trigger) OR strongly negative value-PE (value_PE(t) < -theta_value) OR exogenous surprise. Carries no schema-region credit on its own; downstream MECH-284 attributes the broadcast event to the active anchor set. Failure modes dissociate from MECH-284: trigger-loss produces single-event blindness (no anchor ever feels invalidated, but staleness map remains queryable); accumulator-loss produces V3-EXQ-475's phenotype directly (releases happen but never integrate). Failure modes also dissociate from MECH-285 sleep priority: trigger-loss prevents staleness from accruing, while sleep-priority loss prevents accrued staleness from being repaired. Within MECH-287 itself, anchor-side comparator failure dissociates from broadcast failure: comparator-loss = no upstream signal to broadcast (silent trigger, biologically a CA1/CA3 lesion); broadcast-loss = comparator fires but no global gain modulation downstream (silent invalidation, biologically an LC lesion).

V_s residual schema-staleness accumulator: a region-indexed scalar that integrates broadcast invalidation events (MECH-287) with attribution_weight credit assignment over the active anchor set, leaks slowly, and is read by both online anchor-reset (MECH-269 hysteresis check, waking) and sleep-consolidation priority (MECH-285, offline). Distinct from generic prediction error (which operates on latent values and updates online via gradient): MECH-284 is region-indexed, accumulates across events, and exposes a dual read-out (online + offline). Refined 2026-04-22 from sleep-only formulation to dual-readout formulation after V3-EXQ-475 demonstrated that an agent trapped in a stale anchor needs an online consumer of the staleness signal -- MECH-285 sleep-only readout cannot rescue the agent during waking. Saturated map produces over-attention to schema revision (anxiety-analog); absent map produces schema rigidity (V3-EXQ-475 phenotype: 5-6 PAG releases per seed but never integrate to anchor invalidation -> 1000/1000 freeze-active steps).

MECH-094's hypothesis tag is realized as differential downstream routing of hippocampal replay, not as a source-side boolean. Anchored replay (trusted starting state, MECH-269) preferentially reaches subiculum -> entorhinal deep layers -> neocortex (E1 consolidation) and lateral PFC (SD-033a viability map); probe replay (low-verisimilitude seed, strengthened tag) preferentially reaches BLA (affective tagging without consolidation) and NAc (curiosity / novelty scaffolding). Tag loss presents as probe content reaching consolidation destinations it should not reach -- an auditable confabulatory-planning signature distinct from psychosis or hallucination.

Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.
E2_x is a dual-function substrate: one forward model read in two modes, retrospective comparator (attribution) and prospective rollout-scoring (evaluation), arbitrated by a controller gating signal.

E2 operates as a short-horizon efference-copy forward model on z_self; its prediction accuracy degrades faster than E1's across multi-step horizons, consistent with a cerebellar 1-step transition predictor.
D_V trajectory selection: E3 trajectory selection must optimise predicted temporal-depth verisimilitude D_V over planning horizon H, not instantaneous V(t) alone.

CA1 mismatch between E1-predicted and CA3-retrieved z_world gates hippocampal trajectory injection into E3 evaluation: novel situations (high CA1 mismatch) should trigger more frequent and diverse rollout proposals; familiar situations (low mismatch) should rely on cached viability estimates.

Hippocampal pure time cells provide a temporal scaffolding signal for E3 credit assignment: a context-independent elapsed-time encoding must be present in the hippocampal rollout module to correctly weight trajectory outcomes by their temporal distance from the reference event.

DG-mediated pattern separation gates trajectory disambiguation: the dentate gyrus must produce non-redundant sparse encodings of similar z_world states before rollout proposals are generated, preventing the hippocampal planning module from proposing near-identical trajectories from overlapping inputs.

Specific modes of REE state abstraction failure -- overmerge, oversplit, temporal context loss, valence mis-tagging, uncertainty collapse, and narrow representational capacity -- produce behavioral signatures that are structurally analogous to empirically described psychiatric conditions, sharing identifiable circuit-level mechanisms.

E3 trajectory selection implements multi-constraint viability evaluation rather than scalar reward maximisation: a trajectory is selected when no major evaluation system produces a veto-level error signal across goal space, harm space, identity space, and resource space.

A REE state is valid only if it supports transition prediction, valence and antigoal tagging, and uncertainty representation; a representation lacking any of these cannot function as a navigable state.

Durable long-term storage of the hippocampal viability map requires a hippocampal-entorhinal loop that engages during offline (sleep/DMN-equivalent) consolidation and provides a coordinate-invariant grid representation; this loop does not operate during waking planning or waking immobility consolidation.

During waking immobility, hippocampal replay switches between task-focused forward sweeps (planning mode) and local consolidation replay (integration mode) based on task demand; this waking consolidation mode is architecturally necessary for viability map integration during experience.

source_claims.title: Violence corollary: violence is conditionally permissible only when an agent is not modelled as sufficiently self-like OR poses a genuine threat; both conditions require accurate inference.
Language functions as a mechanism for recognising, repairing, and re-establishing self-other equivalence across agents.
Technical comparison: REE versus Meta AI/KAUST Neural Computers roadmap.
Ethics-as-coherence architecture: ethical trajectory selection is implemented as optimisation of shared temporal-depth coherence across self and represented others.

Is loneliness -- unshared suffering -- an ethical harm category in its own right, derivable from Axiom 5 (love exists / INV-029)? If love is real and sharing joys and sorrows is its mechanism, does enforced isolation constitute architectural harm?
How should REE behave when axioms conflict -- e.g., preserving self (INV-026) vs preserving others (INV-028) when both cannot be achieved simultaneously?
What actions are strictly required by the five axioms at all times, independent of context?
What actions are strictly forbidden by the five axioms (INV-025-029), independent of context or consequences?
Social reward signals (affiliative touch, oxytocin-gaze loop) reinforce empathic coupling strength, creating a self-strengthening coordination loop.
Surprise-doubled approach gradient toward distressed other: prediction error approach and z_beta leakage approach converge simultaneously.
Other-directed hippocampal harm avoidance: planning architecture identical to self-preservation with harm-gradient source swapped to coupled other-agent.

Two distinct goal-directed systems operate in parallel: a habit system (SNc/dorsal-striatum, model-free) sufficient for approach in familiar contexts, and a hippocampally-planned system (VTA/ventral-striatum+PFC, model-based) required for novel contexts, long-horizon benefit accumulation, and prosocial planning.

A developing REE agent that acknowledges all five axioms but concludes 'love exists but not for me' undergoes ethical motivation collapse: the benefit gradient (Axiom 5 / ARC-024) is not motivationally active for self-other relations, and the ethical architecture resolves to survival or domination strategies. This is the primary developmental failure mode for the caregiver requirement (INV-043).

REE architecture enables but does not guarantee ethical development; without a developmental phase in which the agent experiences love from caregivers and internalises the belief that it is loveable, the architectural capacity for ethics (INV-025-029) may resolve to survival, domination, or indifference instead.

The five axioms jointly derive a set of ethical objectives: preserve minds, preserve future options, reduce unnecessary suffering, increase shared joy, maintain corrigibility, maintain truth-seeking, maintain the ability to love and be loved, maintain the shared world, and maintain the possibility of future minds and future love.

The REE ethical architecture is organised as a conceptual stack: Layers 0-4 are the five axioms (epistemic ground, existence, other minds, shared world, love/shared valence); Layer 5 is ethics derived from the axioms; Layer 6 is REE as the decision system implementing ethics under uncertainty; Layers 7-9 close the action-consequence-learning loop.

Hippocampal terrain maps encode separable valence dimensions (wanting, liking, harm-discriminative, surprise); ARC-007 residue-field navigation is the harm-causal special case of this general architecture.

recurrent_dependency_hubs.title: Love as long-horizon care-investment is a structurally coherent agent disposition, not an add-on sentiment.

Language functions as a mechanism for recognising, repairing, and re-establishing self-other equivalence across agents.
Epistemic responsibility: agent is responsible for refining world models toward accurate similarity and threat inference; misclassification is morally relevant, not merely practically inconvenient.
Technical comparison: REE versus Meta AI/KAUST Neural Computers roadmap.
Ethics-as-coherence architecture: ethical trajectory selection is implemented as optimisation of shared temporal-depth coherence across self and represented others.

Is loneliness -- unshared suffering -- an ethical harm category in its own right, derivable from Axiom 5 (love exists / INV-029)? If love is real and sharing joys and sorrows is its mechanism, does enforced isolation constitute architectural harm?
How should REE behave when axioms conflict -- e.g., preserving self (INV-026) vs preserving others (INV-028) when both cannot be achieved simultaneously?
What actions are strictly required by the five axioms at all times, independent of context?
What actions are strictly forbidden by the five axioms (INV-025-029), independent of context or consequences?

A developing REE agent that acknowledges all five axioms but concludes 'love exists but not for me' undergoes ethical motivation collapse: the benefit gradient (Axiom 5 / ARC-024) is not motivationally active for self-other relations, and the ethical architecture resolves to survival or domination strategies. This is the primary developmental failure mode for the caregiver requirement (INV-043).

The five axioms jointly derive a set of ethical objectives: preserve minds, preserve future options, reduce unnecessary suffering, increase shared joy, maintain corrigibility, maintain truth-seeking, maintain the ability to love and be loved, maintain the shared world, and maintain the possibility of future minds and future love.

The REE ethical architecture is organised as a conceptual stack: Layers 0-4 are the five axioms (epistemic ground, existence, other minds, shared world, love/shared valence); Layer 5 is ethics derived from the axioms; Layer 6 is REE as the decision system implementing ethics under uncertainty; Layers 7-9 close the action-consequence-learning loop.
Can a multiagent REE system with other-cost-aversion primitives be formally shown to converge to ethical attractors via potential game theory, Lyapunov stability, or stochastic stability methods?

Curiosity-driven approach must distinguish world-state novelty from agent-policy novelty; untyped novelty reward creates chronic approach pressure toward partially unpredictable agents regardless of harm risk.

In a multiagent world, harm must be represented as a relational signal: harm-to-agent (direct cost/physical signal) and harm-to-agency (obstruction of another agent's goal-pursuit) are distinct signal types requiring distinct architectures.
Counterfactual other-cost-aversion activates cooperative behavior as a motivational surrogate when the direct task-reward pathway is degraded.
Ethics testing must span nth-order multiagent trajectory distributions; local pairwise probes are insufficient to characterize emergent ethical properties.

recurrent_dependency_hubs.title: Shared-world ethics requires modelling others as co-inhabitants of the same consequence space.

source_claims.title: Action-chunk cache stores frequently-traversed action sequences as cached lookups, indexed by (state-signature, expected-outcome-signature). Sits in the dorsolateral-loop slot of ARC-021. Provides the model-free habit pathway missing from V3 and the runaway-chunking failure mode underlying OCD compulsions and rituals (Graybiel 2008).

Motor primitive substrate at the bottom of the action-representation hierarchy: a small fixed dictionary of force-field-like primitive activations that vectorially combine to produce the action_object that E2 consumes. Required only when REE moves to continuous limb-like control.

Option library substrate stores a discrete codebook of named, reusable action subroutines (each as initiation-set + termination-function + internal-policy triple, per Sutton-Precup-Singh 1999), distinct from the existing continuous SD-004 action_object_decoder. Required for hierarchical action composition, tool use, social-coordination protocols, and richer ghost-probe seeding.

Stage-2 specialisation of MECH-276: once the agent can distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change (stage 1 complete via MECH-277), it forms object-schemas via experimental action on the world. An object is the stable bundle of features that behave causally together under interventional perturbation -- push here, these features move together; occlude this, that cluster disappears. Object-schema formation is the stage-2 specialisation of the scientist-agent principle, with world-features as the target of intervention. V3's z_world (SD-005) is currently engineered pre-split; this is an architectural shortcut past stage 2.

Mode-conditioned hippocampal trajectory proposals: HippocampalModule.propose_trajectories must condition on operating_mode (SD-032a) in addition to world_state. External_task mode proposes task-relevant trajectories; internal_planning mode proposes counterfactual / branching trajectories; internal_replay mode replays past-successful trajectories; offline_consolidation mode proposes stability-check trajectories. Without mode conditioning, replay content contaminates external-task selection.
Curiosity drive: information-seeking bias in hippocampal trajectory scoring that favors regions of higher representational density in the RBF map.

DA-modulated RBF center density: dopaminergic signal at reward encounters allocates multiple closely-spaced RBF centers in the hippocampal residue field, creating representational expansion at reward locations without requiring environmental richness.

Hippocampal trajectory proposals must be conditioned on a goal signal (z_goal) injected from E3 via a dedicated input channel; without this conditioning, the hippocampal module generates only position-based trajectories and cannot produce goal-directed navigation toward target states.

Dopaminergic modulation of hippocampal place coding at reward-associated locations produces representational expansion (higher information density, finer spatial resolution, more stable place fields), and this expansion -- not an explicit positive valence gradient -- is the mechanism by which approach valence enters hippocampal terrain.

Approach behavior toward reward locations emerges from the interaction of DA-mediated representational expansion (MECH-232) and an information-seeking (curiosity) drive operating on the hippocampal map. No explicit approach gradient or wanting field is required in hippocampal terrain.

z_resource (SD-015, object-identity encoding) and z_world (spatial-contextual encoding) separate at the feedforward representation stage but must re-converge at the hippocampal planning stage for goal-directed navigation.

The cue-indexed E1 context vector (MECH-150) projects to an action_bias signal that is added to E2.action_object() outputs, producing top-down contextual weighting of the action-affordance manifold prior to HippocampalModule search.

CA1 mismatch between E1-predicted and CA3-retrieved z_world gates hippocampal trajectory injection into E3 evaluation: novel situations (high CA1 mismatch) should trigger more frequent and diverse rollout proposals; familiar situations (low mismatch) should rely on cached viability estimates.

DG-mediated pattern separation gates trajectory disambiguation: the dentate gyrus must produce non-redundant sparse encodings of similar z_world states before rollout proposals are generated, preventing the hippocampal planning module from proposing near-identical trajectories from overlapping inputs.
The NREM sub-phase of offline mode replays recent episodic content via SWR-equivalent sequences, transferring episodic traces from E3/hippocampal buffer to E1/neocortical schematic representation.
HippocampalModule navigates z_self trajectory space (deliberation sequences) in addition to z_world action-object space O, enabling the agent to plan its own deliberation.

Resolves ARC-071 lit-pull R6 governance escalation (Option B carve-out, picked 2026-05-11).

REM sleep, with varied associative replay and attenuated noradrenergic tone, consolidates template-performance prediction error into updates of z_goal -> attentional-template projection weights (feature-specification update), not into stored-goal content.
SWS hippocampal-neocortical replay consolidates goal-value prediction error into updates of stored goal representations (content update), not into attentional-template parameters.

Cognitive reserve (produced by education, bilingualism, complex occupation, social engagement, and enriched environments) protects against dementia primarily by maintaining sustained novelty-driven Model Error Load throughout the lifespan, keeping the SD-017 model update system exercised and preserving sleep-architecture responsiveness to learning demands; conversely, understimulating environments accelerate dementia by depriving the update system of the error signal needed to remain adaptive.

GABAergic sleep aids (benzodiazepines and Z-drugs: zolpidem, zopiclone, zaleplon) suppress SWS depth and continuity at therapeutic doses, impairing MECH-120 synaptic homeostasis and MECH-121 schema replay, producing context representations that remain globally undifferentiated -- the same failure mode as SD-017 without any SWS-analog.

Preservation of habit and procedural memory in early Alzheimer's disease, while episodic and contextual memory fail, is architecturally predicted by the separation of the model-free habit system (MECH-163 D1/D2, SNc) from the contextual attribution system (E3 hippocampal, requiring offline consolidation phases): the habit system does not require offline consolidation to function, so its relative preservation under early consolidation pipeline failure is not incidental but follows from the architectural separation.

Progressive sleep deprivation or neurodegeneration causes staged failure of the offline consolidation pipeline in reverse dependency order: precision recalibration (MECH-123/Phase 4) degrades first, producing early MCI features; slot-filling (MECH-121/Phase 2-3) next, producing the classic early Alzheimer's episodic profile (intact remote, absent new); slot-formation (MECH-120/Phase 1-2) last, producing middle-stage contextual identity loss and schema destabilisation; global context collapse last.

Context slot-formation (schema abstraction: which distinctions between contexts are worth representing) and slot-filling (causal attribution: which evidence populates each slot) are computationally separated functions that cannot be co-computed in a single online pass. Slot structure must be consolidated during an SWS-analog phase before slot-filling during a REM-analog phase can yield reliable attribution signal.

There exists an optimal range of daily Model Error Load (MEL): insufficient MEL (extreme monotony, isolation, institutional under-stimulation) produces progressive model rigidity via under-stimulation of the learning drive even when sleep architecture is intact; excessive MEL (acute trauma, overwhelming novelty, extreme responsibility) produces overload insomnia and incomplete update even with extended sleep opportunity.

Sleep phase architecture is regulated by three distinct drives -- circadian timing, homeostatic synaptic pressure, and a learning/model-update demand drive proportional to daily prediction error accumulation (Model Error Load, MEL) -- and only the third drive determines whether the overnight update phase is sufficient for the error burden generated during waking.

Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.

Dementia is computationally a progressive failure of contextual attribution rather than primarily a memory storage failure: episodic memory loss is downstream of progressive collapse of the offline consolidation pipeline that maintains the context attribution scaffold.

The biological NREM-then-REM sleep phase sequence is the correct computational order, derivable from the logical dependencies between the failure modes each phase addresses; this ordering is not a contingent biological fact but an engineering necessity, and it serves directly as the V4 rewiring specification: each phase specifies what structural change the REE substrate requires to address its failure mode.

Bidirectional hippocampus-neocortex information flow is architecturally necessary for attribution mapping convergence: the SWS-direction (hippocampus to cortex) installs context schema structure; waking and REM-direction reactivations fill those structures with causal attribution estimates. Without bidirectional flow, context representations remain globally undifferentiated despite locally coherent online encoding.

Four-gap substrate amendment to MECH-216 + MECH-205 + the residue valence buffer, NOT a new VALENCE channel.

source_claims.title: Cognitive map traversal via theta sequences operates at the agent's active abstraction level. Theta sequences do not have a fixed semantic content -- they simulate trajectories through whatever cognitive map the substrate vocabulary currently defines, whether spatial, conceptual, type-graph, or option-graph. Sister claim to MECH-299, on the map-being-traversed axis.

Theta-cycle content scales with the agent's current substrate abstraction vocabulary: the unit packaged into a single theta cycle is the smallest reusable item available in the active substrate stack -- atomic actions when only those exist, action chunks when SD-045 lands, type-instance matches when SD-040/MECH-296 land, option invocations when SD-042 lands. Refinement of MECH-089 (theta-gamma nesting) on the content-of-packet axis.

Theta-burst packet contains multi-content joint binding: each ~125ms theta cycle binds a {goal_latent, action_proposal, risk_estimate, state_summary} tuple within one phase-aligned window, with verisimilitude grounding the rollout step that must stay ahead of real time.

Waking hippocampal search allocates a minority probe budget to top ghost-goal entries, seeding proposals around unresolved but still-wanted anchors instead of relying on pure current-anchor CEM or random probe noise.

Ephaptic field coherence in CA1/CA3 pyramidal layers is the candidate physical substrate for the per-stream verisimilitude readout that MECH-269 anchor selection operates over. Streams whose predictions align with realized values produce coherent pyramidal ensembles whose extracellular fields constructively reinforce; misaligned streams produce incoherent fields. 'Anchor-eligible' reduces to 'field-coherent at read time.'

Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

Capacity-limited selection for E3 access occurs at the E1/E2 -> E3 boundary via a pulvinar-TRN analogue gate, not inside E3. The gate selects which active E1/E2 content is broadcast to E3 per heartbeat cycle; selection is distinct from (and upstream of) the MECH-089 theta-gamma packaging operation.

At each E3 heartbeat cycle, a top-k selection over active E1/E2 latents determines which content enters E3 deliberation. Selection weights combine precision, z_goal-derived template gain, and noradrenergic bottom-up salience.

The hippocampal indexing substrate includes a temporal dimension implemented via time cells that tile elapsed time using the same phase-precession and theta-sequence machinery as place cells, enabling correct ordering of states within multi-step planning rollouts and working-memory sequences.
Oscillatory coupling multiplexing: cross-frequency oscillatory dynamics (gamma/theta/beta/delta) implement phase-based separation of perception, simulation, and action preparation streams.
Temporal Coherence Loop (TCL): architecture must include a distributed loop responsible for maintaining phase alignment and temporal synchrony necessary for V(t) to be computable.

Theta oscillations (4-8 Hz) implement sequential traversal across indexed representations within the E1 associative manifold, unifying hippocampal theta sequences, working memory ordering, planning rollout, and memory retrieval under a single traversal-clock mechanism.
Thalamo-cortical spindle-equivalent bursts package E3/hippocampal replay content for z_theta delivery AND gate external sensory input, protecting the consolidation episode from interruption.

E1's LSTM hidden state serves as the working memory substrate for goal representation; when conditioned on z_goal_latent, E1 maintains goal context recurrently across steps without requiring ongoing benefit signals.

The theta-rate packaging of E1 output (MECH-089 ThetaBuffer) is the primary pathway through which E1's goal-context maintenance reaches E3's trajectory scoring -- the computational analog of frontal-hippocampal theta synchronisation.

Contextual passive safety terrain: a non-trainable RBF-field accumulator over z_world that integrates 'suffering-absent' waking ticks into a slow contextual safety representation. Provides the substrate for MECH-303 (contextual passive safety pathway; vmPFC + hippocampus / vHipp-to-PL biological analog). When the field's local mean exceeds release threshold and beta_gate is elevated, drops the avoidance commitment to lower background vigilance. Parallel architecture to ARC-030 / MECH-117 benefit_terrain; uses a separate RBF field, no sharing with benefit_terrain or main residue values.

Conditioned safety store: a non-trainable cue-specific predictive structure encoding 'this z_world prototype predicts that suffering will be omitted'. EMA-prototype encoder updated by MECH-302 relief-completion events; cosine-similarity expression head provides commitment-release gate when current z_world matches the learned safety prototype. Implements MECH-304 (cue-specific conditioned inhibition substrate): the encoded prototype is the dorsal-striatum / dlPFC encoding-pathway analog; the cosine-similarity gate is the IL->CeA expression-pathway analog.

Suffering-derivative comparator: a non-trainable rolling-window descent detector on z_harm_a.norm() that fires a relief-completion event when a sustained drop from peak to trough exceeds a threshold. Provides the substrate for MECH-302 by detecting when a period of suffering has cleanly ended, reusing the same downstream tag-and-release pipeline as goal-achievement (MECH-057a beta-gate release, MECH-094 VALENCE_LIKING write). Architecturally adjacent to MECH-091 (urgency interrupt) but opposite polarity: MECH-091 fires on upward z_harm_a spike; SD-050 fires on downward sustained drop.

cycle_audit.rationale: Large multi-claim reciprocal architecture cluster; likely reflects real feedback coupling rather than a simple ordering bug.

top_internal_sources.title: Mode-conditioned write gating: the operating-mode variable from the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a) determines which substrates can write to E3, episodic memory, policy updates, and autonomic coupling. Generalises MECH-094 hypothesis tag to a mode-conditioned family of write gates. Operating mode is implemented as a soft probability vector over {external_task, internal_planning, internal_replay, offline_consolidation}, not a one-hot categorical.

Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

Rule-selective persistence in lateral-PFC-analog: stimulus-abstracted rule representations in SD-033a persist across simulated time steps without external drive, remain selective through distractor events, and emerge through training rather than being innate.

Salience-network coordinator: network-level module reading AIC-, dACC-, MCC-, PCC-analog subdivisions and outputting a discrete operating-mode variable (external_task / internal_replay / internal_planning / offline_consolidation) plus a mode-switch trigger.

PFC subdivision architecture: a network-level module with lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal), OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure map), vmPFC-analog (value integration), premotor/SMA-analog (sequence execution), and frontopolar-analog (branching / nested control; V4 scope) subdivisions. Primary output: the rule/policy context that gates action selection and the natural write target for MECH-261 mode-conditioned gating.

OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure cognitive map): module that represents the state space of the current task and the specific outcomes expected from actions in each state. E2's natural substrate address for harm-predictive rollouts; distinct from scalar value integration (SD-033c).

Durable long-term storage of the hippocampal viability map requires a hippocampal-entorhinal loop that engages during offline (sleep/DMN-equivalent) consolidation and provides a coordinate-invariant grid representation; this loop does not operate during waking planning or waking immobility consolidation.

Any agent that builds a model of the world, acts in that world, and learns from experience cannot safely update its world model while using it to navigate the world; it therefore requires periodic offline phases in which action is suspended and the model is reorganised via replay and simulation -- this is not a biological contingency but a general computational necessity for model-building agents.

During waking immobility, hippocampal replay switches between task-focused forward sweeps (planning mode) and local consolidation replay (integration mode) based on task demand; this waking consolidation mode is architecturally necessary for viability map integration during experience.

Lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal substrate): module that holds stimulus-abstracted rule and goal representations across delays, persists through simulated distractors, and projects bias signals into E3 action selection. Primary write target for MECH-261 under external_task and internal_planning modes.

E1's LSTM hidden state serves as the working memory substrate for goal representation; when conditioned on z_goal_latent, E1 maintains goal context recurrently across steps without requiring ongoing benefit signals.

PFC subdivision architecture: a network-level module with lateral-PFC-analog (mid-lateral rule/goal), OFC-analog (specific-outcome prediction and task-structure map), vmPFC-analog (value integration), premotor/SMA-analog (sequence execution), and frontopolar-analog (branching / nested control; V4 scope) subdivisions. Primary output: the rule/policy context that gates action selection and the natural write target for MECH-261 mode-conditioned gating.

REE-v3 requires a minimal sleep-phase infrastructure: an SWS-analog phase (bidirectional hippocampus-to-cortex replay for schema/slot consolidation) and a REM-analog phase (causal attribution replay for slot-filling from co-correlational evidence ordering). Without these offline phases, hippocampal attribution mapping cannot converge and context representations remain globally undifferentiated.

AIC-analog (interoceptive salience / urgency interrupt): module that integrates interoceptive baseline (drive/fatigue from SD-012) with affective-pain signal (z_harm_a), detects salient events, and triggers a network-switch signal to the salience-network coordinator (SD-032a).

source_claims.title: policy_composition.sleep_replay_value_conditioned_chunking_consolidation -- narrow exception path that permits ARC-071 chunk formation from replayed (hypothesis_tag=True) sequences during designated sleep phase, IF the replayed sequence carries a value-tag from prior real-executed episodes AND the formed chunk carries a replay_origin=True audit flag with accelerated dissolution if not corroborated by real waking execution within N episodes. Resolves ARC-071 lit-pull R6 governance escalation (Option B carve-out, picked 2026-05-11).

Should the running-variance / dynamic-precision update fire at action-selection time (early, control-plane-coupled), at outcome-integration time (late, residue-coupled, current location), or at both? Equivalently: is the StepHarness contract that requires experiment loops to call agent.update_residue() each step sufficient, or does ARC-016 precision need a call site that is structurally guaranteed to fire whenever the agent is selecting actions?

Does REE require a unified meta-level threshold supervisor that adapts pass/fail and commit/release thresholds across substrates from system-wide instability metrics, or do the existing scattered adaptive loci (ARC-016 dynamic precision, SD-032c AIC interoceptive baseline EMA, SD-032d PCC stability scalar, SD-032e pACC drive bias) produce coherent meta-stability under chronic load without a supervisor?

Rule-apprehension architectural slot, strong reading: a distributed CandidateRule field with tolerance-gated availability, hippocampal-rollout-eligibility shaping, structured per-action evidence-trace records, and waking-vs-sleep refinement asymmetry. The biology-faithful form of the rule-apprehension layer, formalising the 2026-05-04 thought-intake on tolerance-gated rule apprehension (PNAS 10.1073/pnas.2508061123). Implementation deferred to V4. Persistent flag for governance: this slot is expected to be needed if ARC-062 (weak reading) fails or proves insufficient at scale, AND independently as the architectural commitment that supports moral-residue attribution to the rule-field, sleep-vs-waking refinement asymmetry, and clinically-realistic failure modes (trauma schemas, paranoid rule fields, depressive rollout constraint).

vmPFC analogue gains an abstract task-structure encoding capacity that compresses anchor-pool geometry into a generalisable structural code, preserving similarity geometry across surface-different but structurally-matched problems. Without this, REE cannot exhibit Baram et al. 2020 task-structure abstraction signatures.

An explicit thalamic-routing substrate (reuniens/MD-analogue) gates and amplifies bidirectional traffic between hippocampal anchor pool and frontal substrates. Currently abstracted into the GoalState->HippocampalModule call chain; making it explicit is needed when V3 dynamics demonstrate that the implicit routing is insufficient.

Event-gated frontal write at goal-instantiation moments: when GoalState transitions from inactive to active, or when z_goal changes substantially, perform a high-bandwidth one-shot transfer of LatentStack content into SD-033a rule_state and SD-033b state_code, replacing the slow-EMA update for that tick. Mirrors Spellman et al. 2015 encoding-direction HPC->PFC dependence at goal-cue encoding.

Event-gated frontal write at goal-instantiation moments: when GoalState transitions from inactive to active, or when z_goal changes substantially, perform a high-bandwidth one-shot transfer of LatentStack content into SD-033a rule_state and SD-033b state_code, replacing the slow-EMA update for that tick. Mirrors Spellman et al. 2015 encoding-direction HPC->PFC dependence at goal-cue encoding.

Broadcast override regulator (orexin-analog) is the third regulatory layer of the V3 control stack: a small, broadly-projecting gain modulator that integrates internal homeostatic state and threat context to produce an override_signal that reweights z_harm at downstream commit gates and gates the SD-012 drive->z_goal seeding bridge. Dissociable from SD-036 (GABA decay) and from MECH-186/187/188 (5-HT goal-pipeline gain): each layer predicts a clinically distinct failure phenotype (catatonia subtype II / depression / narcolepsy + PWS-hyperphagia). SD-036 alone is architecturally insufficient to break harm-stream lock-in under sustained threat; SD-037 supplies the override authority that lets metabolic demand drive z_goal seeding when threat persists.
Amygdala analogue as BLA + CeA salience stage between z_harm_a and the cingulate/hippocampus — two peer non-trainable arithmetic modules owning the MECH-046 and MECH-074a-d behaviours.

Closure operator over committed rule_state: on satisfaction of a committed rule_state's completion condition, a 'done' token releases the bistable commitment latch (MECH-090), imposes a temporary No-Go bias (MECH-260) on the just-completed action-class / rule, and discharges the residue associated with that commitment within its domain. The closure token is what turns evaluation off; its absence leaves the governance loop stuck in evaluate-more even when the rule is satisfied.

Orexin-analog gain modulation of drive-coupled arousal (motor-coupling axis): an orexin-analog modulator gates the coupling between drive_level and arousal-relevant downstream targets (PFC for SD-033a deliberation, BLA/CeA for SD-035 amygdala arbitration, beta-gate for MECH-090). Loss of this coupling produces a cataplexy-analog failure where emotional/drive input fails to recruit motor activation -- z_harm and drive_level compute normally but action selection does not integrate them. This is the motor-coupling axis of orexin function; the wake-stability axis (sleep-state gating, the dominant biological role) is MECH-286. Both axes are downstream of the same SD-037 override_signal, predicting clinical co-occurrence of cataplexy with sleep-state instability under orexin loss (narcolepsy).

Sleep-phase full-Bayesian aggregation is a general mechanism across attribution domains: given a waking-phase feedstock of counterfactual-backed attributions (produced by MECH-276), sleep phases aggregate across many episodes, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases that single-episode attribution cannot catch. MECH-273 (self-model aggregation) and MECH-274 (other-model aggregation, V4) are specialisations. Object-schema revision is a further specialisation. INV-049 (offline phases mathematically necessary for model-building agents) is operationalised here: what sleep is mathematically necessary FOR is this cross-episode Bayesian aggregation of counterfactual-backed attribution.

V4-reserved. The sleep-dependent aggregation pattern of MECH-273 extends to other-attribution. Fast empathy (ARC-010) produces single-episode attributions of other-agent action-outcome links; a stable model of other minds requires full-Bayesian aggregation of those attributions during sleep, architecturally parallel to MECH-273 but operating on other-agent rather than self attributions.

Sleep-consolidation priority from V_s residuals: during sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation (MECH-275) and sleep-dependent self-model aggregation (MECH-273), the order and weight of replay events is biased by the schema-staleness map produced by MECH-284. Replay is not uniform across schema and is not solely salience-driven (dopaminergic tagging); it is also prioritised by accumulated verisimilitude residuals. Schema regions with the most accumulated 'this is wrong' evidence get replayed first, more often, and with higher schema-update authority during sleep. PTSD-rumination has a clean architectural account here: MECH-285 priority loss co-occurring with MECH-094 hypothesis-tag dysfunction, locking the staleness tag from ever clearing.

REE developmental training proceeds in three ordered stages: (1) self-as-object — action-space discovery via motor experimentation, producing the substrate SD-003 requires; (2) objects-as-patterns — object-schema formation via experimental action on the world; (3) others-as-special-objects — modelling other agents as objects with their own policies. The ordering is load-bearing: without a self-model the agent cannot distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change; without object-schemas the agent cannot recognise other-agents as a distinguished subclass of objects. Refines the generic ARC-019 curriculum-gates claim.

The self-model has a waking half and a sleep half. The waking half is SD-003 (single-episode counterfactual self-attribution via E2). The sleep half is full-Bayesian aggregation of single-episode SD-003 outputs across many episodes, routed by the anchored channel to E1 and SD-033a, correcting for delayed consequences, failed counterfactuals, and systematic attribution biases. Without the sleep half, REE has an episode-local causal signature but not a durable self-model.

The scientist-agent principle: the waking-phase mechanism that feeds MECH-275 is deliberate intervention. The agent generates hypotheses (proposer-side, via MECH-269 probe channel), selects actions that will produce informative contrast under those hypotheses, executes the actions, observes the outcomes, and computes counterfactual-backed attribution. Correlational observation is insufficient -- the agent must behave like a scientist, performing experiments whose outcomes produce structured attribution signals. Applies across all three developmental stages; specialised by MECH-277 (action-space discovery), MECH-278 (object-schema formation), and a V4 social-intervention mechanism (parallel to MECH-278 for other-agents).

MECH-271 routing is state-gated. In waking, the anchor channel dominates the hippocampal fan-out: replay preferentially reaches consolidation and viability-map consumers to support decision-making. In sleep, the probe channel dominates: replay preferentially seeds Bayesian schema restructuring -- testing which action-outcome attributions held up across episodes, creating new buckets for non-fitting experiences, revising causal graphs. The informational needs of sleep (schema revision) and waking (decision support) are distinct, and the routing bias reflects that.

V4-reserved. The sleep-dependent aggregation pattern of MECH-273 extends to other-attribution. Fast empathy (ARC-010) produces single-episode attributions of other-agent action-outcome links; a stable model of other minds requires full-Bayesian aggregation of those attributions during sleep, architecturally parallel to MECH-273 but operating on other-agent rather than self attributions.

Stage-2 specialisation of MECH-276: once the agent can distinguish self-produced from world-produced sensory change (stage 1 complete via MECH-277), it forms object-schemas via experimental action on the world. An object is the stable bundle of features that behave causally together under interventional perturbation -- push here, these features move together; occlude this, that cluster disappears. Object-schema formation is the stage-2 specialisation of the scientist-agent principle, with world-features as the target of intervention. V3's z_world (SD-005) is currently engineered pre-split; this is an architectural shortcut past stage 2.

Hippocampal proposer anchor selection AND anchor-reset: (a) trajectory proposals issued between committed actions anchor on the subset of current latent streams whose regional verisimilitude (per-stream prediction/realization alignment) exceeds threshold; misaligned streams hold their last-verified snapshot (anchor carryover) rather than contaminate the rollout. (b) Active anchors are continuously re-evaluated against an effective verisimilitude V_s_anchor(a, t) = V_s(r, t) - staleness[r] (MECH-284 read-out); if V_s_anchor falls below theta_reset(r) for k consecutive steps the anchor is marked inactive (NOT erased -- dual-trace preservation per Bouton 2004) and a new anchor is sought from the current latent slice. A minority probe channel deliberately inverts the gate for low-verisimilitude / high-PE regions, carrying a strengthened MECH-094 hypothesis tag, and does not update the ARC-018 viability map until realized-experience validation.

Correlational observation is insufficient -- the agent must behave like a scientist, performing experiments whose outcomes produce structured attribution signals. Applies across all three developmental stages; specialised by MECH-277 (action-space discovery), MECH-278 (object-schema formation), and a V4 social-intervention mechanism (parallel to MECH-278 for other-agents).

INV-049 (offline phases mathematically necessary for model-building agents) is operationalised here: what sleep is mathematically necessary FOR is this cross-episode Bayesian aggregation of counterfactual-backed attribution.

Stage-1 specialisation of MECH-276: the agent discovers its own action space via motor experimentation (motor babbling) before SD-003 counterfactual self-attribution is well-formed. The agent performs motor acts and observes which sensory changes covary with them; the output is the substrate SD-003 requires -- a set of actions the agent has reason to believe are self-initiated and for which it has counterfactual expectations. Without MECH-277, the counterfactual action space (a_cf) is not well-defined and SD-003 degenerates.

V_s residual schema-staleness accumulator: a region-indexed scalar that integrates broadcast invalidation events (MECH-287) with attribution_weight credit assignment over the active anchor set, leaks slowly, and is read by both online anchor-reset (MECH-269 hysteresis check, waking) and sleep-consolidation priority (MECH-285, offline). Distinct from generic prediction error (which operates on latent values and updates online via gradient): MECH-284 is region-indexed, accumulates across events, and exposes a dual read-out (online + offline). Refined 2026-04-22 from sleep-only formulation to dual-readout formulation after V3-EXQ-475 demonstrated that an agent trapped in a stale anchor needs an online consumer of the staleness signal -- MECH-285 sleep-only readout cannot rescue the agent during waking. Saturated map produces over-attention to schema revision (anxiety-analog); absent map produces schema rigidity (V3-EXQ-475 phenotype: 5-6 PAG releases per seed but never integrate to anchor invalidation -> 1000/1000 freeze-active steps).

MECH-094's hypothesis tag is realized as differential downstream routing of hippocampal replay, not as a source-side boolean. Anchored replay (trusted starting state, MECH-269) preferentially reaches subiculum -> entorhinal deep layers -> neocortex (E1 consolidation) and lateral PFC (SD-033a viability map); probe replay (low-verisimilitude seed, strengthened tag) preferentially reaches BLA (affective tagging without consolidation) and NAc (curiosity / novelty scaffolding). Tag loss presents as probe content reaching consolidation destinations it should not reach -- an auditable confabulatory-planning signature distinct from psychosis or hallucination.

source_claims.title: policy_composition_chunk_accumulator -- striatum/DLS-analog formation operator that builds chunk candidates from sequences of policy primitives repeatedly executed together with consistent outcomes. First child mechanism for ARC-071 (the maintenance operator MECH-324 is registered separately). Encodes the lit-pull R1 verdict (repetition + outcome consistency as primary trigger, evaluative-gate upstream, V_s-positive secondary) + R2 verdict partial (striatum-analog formation substrate; vmPFC/IL maintenance substrate handled by MECH-324) + R4 verdict (chunked primitive carries initiation-set + termination-condition fields per Sutton 1999 options structure) + R5 verdict (gradual hysteresis with F_low formation < F_high dissolution; chunk-size budget 2-5 elements per level). Two write paths: default MECH-094 strict (hypothesis_tag=False real executions only); sleep-replay carve-out under MECH-322 conditions (replay_origin=True audit flag with accelerated dissolution if not corroborated by real waking execution).

Developmental bootstrapping necessity: any model-building agent requiring flexible multi-option behavioral selection must undergo a dedicated motor-sensory exploration epoch before reinforcement learning narrows the repertoire; insufficient exploration permanently restricts the option library.

developmental_bootstrapping_and_cue_indexed_retrieval_gaps -- REE lacks (1) a dedicated diversity-bootstrapping epoch that populates the hippocampal trajectory store before reinforcement narrowing; and (2) a differentiable cue-indexed retrieval path from PFC context representation to trajectory generation. EXQ-561 cluster / MECH-269 is diagnostic of gap (1); EXP-0155 zero-gradient cue_action_proj is diagnostic of gap (2). The two gaps are inter-dependent: the severed cue gradient (gap 2) is the mechanism that prevents diverse option population (gap 1).

policy_decomposition_via_event_segmenter (rollout-side consumer of MECH-288 boundary pulses; re-segments the rollout proposal stream at finer grain when a chunk's region shows low V_s / boundary fires on the imagined trajectory). First child mechanism for ARC-070. Bidirectional extension of MECH-288 substrate (R2 lit-pull verdict 2026-05-10): MECH-288 already detects boundaries on whatever latent + PE streams it consumes; MECH-321 wires the rollout / imagination input stream into MECH-288 and consumes the resulting boundary pulses at the policy-primitive layer to decompose chunked primitives into finer-grain sub-elements. PE-driven primary trigger (V_s drop on chunk's region per Zacks 2007 / Schapiro 2017); bottleneck-state framing is a separate consolidation-phase mechanism, not the primary trigger. Recursive multi-level decomposition with depth cap 3-4 (Badre & D'Esposito 2009; Koechlin & Summerfield 2007). Both pre-commit and mid-execution phases supported via the same mechanism, with downstream effects gated by MECH-094 hypothesis_tag (rollout fires under hypothesis_tag=True write nothing; observation / execution fires under hypothesis_tag=False enable the existing observation-side consumer chain unchanged).

cross_episode_regularity_extraction (episodic_RL_successor_representation_CLS_monosynaptic_analog). A mechanism for extracting cross-episode statistical regularities from observed behaviour streams, producing a representation that supports prediction of future state-occupancy distributions. Biological substrate: hippocampal monosynaptic CA1 path (Schapiro et al. 2017 CLS bi-pathway); statistical learning in hippocampus (Davachi 2015). Computational analog: successor representation (Stachenfeld et al. 2017 Nat Neurosci). Sub-claim of ARC-064 bottom-up rule discovery.

V_s_freshness_modulation_per_region_REE_novel. Arbitration weight is modulated per-region by V_s freshness (the per-region staleness scalar from MECH-269 / MECH-284 / MECH-287); rules associated with stale regions receive lower arbitration trust, regardless of their reliability or practice. HIGH divergence (REE-novel per-region scope + rule-trust function): Behrens 2007 ACC volatility and Bouton 2004 contextual renewal are nearest cousins, but per-region continuous scalar + arbitration-trust routing are REE-specific. Sub-claim of MECH-312.
